teachers.shortBio: A. H. Almaas is the pen name of A. Hameed Ali, an American writer and spiritual teacher who writes about and teaches an approach to spiritual development informed by modern psychol
biography: A. H. Almaas is the pen name of A. Hameed Ali, an American writer and spiritual teacher who writes about and teaches an approach to spiritual development informed by modern psychology and therapy which he calls the Diamond Approach. "Almaas" is the Arabic word for "diamond". Almaas is originally from Kuwait. He is the spiritual head of the Ridhwan School.

teachers.name: Ācārya Malcolm Smith
teachers.shortBio: Ācārya Malcolm Smith is a scholar, translator, and Dzogchen teacher whose main teachers include Chögyal Namkhai Norbu, Kunzang Dechen Lingpa, and Taklung Tsetrul Rinpoche.
biography: Malcolm Smith has studied the Great Perfection since 1992 and completed a traditional three-year solitary retreat. He was awarded the ācārya degree by the Sakya Institute in 2004 and graduated from the Shang Shung Institute School of Tibetan Medicine in 2009.

He has translated major Dzogchen texts including Buddhahood in This Life and the Self-Arisen and Self-Liberated Vidyā Tantras for Wisdom Publications. He teaches and writes on Dzogchen through the Wisdom Experience, Zangthal, and his Substack One Buddhist View.

teachers.name: Adi Shankara
teachers.shortBio: Adi Shankara, also called Adi Shankaracharya, was an Indian Vedic scholar-monk, philosopher, and teacher (acharya) of Advaita Vedanta. He wrote influential commentaries on the Brah
biography: Adi Shankara, also called Adi Shankaracharya, was an Indian Vedic scholar-monk, philosopher, and teacher (acharya) of Advaita Vedanta. He wrote influential commentaries on the Brahma sutras and other texts, and in recent times is often revered as the most important Indian philosopher. Reliable information on Shankara's actual life is scant, and the historical influence of his works on Hindu intellectual thought has been questioned. The historical Shankara was probably relatively unknown and Vaishnava-oriented and his true impact lies in the popular perception of him as a heroic religious leader who re-established traditional Hinduism.

teachers.name: Adyashanti
teachers.shortBio: Adyashanti is an American former spiritual teacher and author from the San Francisco Bay Area who offered talks, online study courses, and retreats in the United States and abroad.
biography: Adyashanti is an American former spiritual teacher and author from the San Francisco Bay Area who offered talks, online study courses, and retreats in the United States and abroad. He is the author of numerous books, CDs and DVDs and, together with his wife Mukti, is the founder of Open Gate Sangha, Inc., a nonprofit organization established in 1996 which supports and makes available his teachings.

teachers.name: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi
teachers.shortBio: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi (1760–1837) was a Moroccan Sunni Islamic scholar, jurist and Sufi, active in Morocco, the Hejaz, Egypt, and Yemen. His main concern was the revivification o
biography: Ahmad ibn Idris al-Fasi (1760–1837) was a Moroccan Sunni Islamic scholar, jurist and Sufi, active in Morocco, the Hejaz, Egypt, and Yemen. His main concern was the revivification of the Sunnah or practice of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, and purifying Islam by erasing Bid'ah and Shirk. His followers founded a number of Sufi paths which spread his teachings across the Muslim world.

teachers.name: Ajahn Brahm
teachers.shortBio: Phra Visuddhisamvarathera AM, known as Ajahn Brahmavaṃso, or simply Ajahn Brahm, is a British-born Buddhist monk. Ordained in 1974, he trained in the Thai Forest Tradition of Thera
biography: Phra Visuddhisamvarathera AM, known as Ajahn Brahmavaṃso, or simply Ajahn Brahm, is a British-born Buddhist monk. Ordained in 1974, he trained in the Thai Forest Tradition of Theravada Buddhism under his teacher Ajahn Chah. Currently, Ajahn Brahm is the abbot of Bodhinyana Monastery in Serpentine, Western Australia, as well as an adviser or patron of various Buddhist organizations in Australia, Singapore, and the UK.

teachers.name: Ajahn Chah
teachers.shortBio: Ajahn Chah was a Thai Buddhist monk. He was an influential teacher of the Buddhadhamma and a founder of two major monasteries in the Thai Forest Tradition.
biography: Ajahn Chah was a Thai Buddhist monk. He was an influential teacher of the Buddhadhamma and a founder of two major monasteries in the Thai Forest Tradition.

teachers.name: Ajahn Sumedho
teachers.shortBio: Ajahn Sumedho is an American Buddhist monk. He was ordained in 1967, and was instrumental in establishing Wat Pa Nanachat in Thailand and the Cittaviveka and Amaravati monasteries
biography: Ajahn Sumedho is an American Buddhist monk. He was ordained in 1967, and was instrumental in establishing Wat Pa Nanachat in Thailand and the Cittaviveka and Amaravati monasteries in England. One of the most senior Western representatives of the Thai Forest Tradition of Theravāda Buddhism, Sumedho is considered a seminal figure in the transmission of the Buddha's teachings to the West.

bibliography.title: Mindfulness : the path to the deathless

teachers.name: Akilesh Ayyar
teachers.shortBio: Akilesh Ayyar is a spiritual mentor steeped in Advaita Vedanta, offering a modern interpretation of Sri Ramana Maharshi's path and emphasizing surrender and self-inquiry.
biography: Akilesh Ayyar is a spiritual mentor in the Advaita Vedanta tradition, offering a contemporary reading of Sri Ramana Maharshi's path.
His teaching emphasizes the twin practices of surrender and self-inquiry as doorways to recognizing one's true nature.

teachers.name: Al-Ghazali
teachers.shortBio: Al-Ghazali, (Persian: ابو حامد محمد ابن محمد غزالی توسی, romanized: Abū Ḥāmid Muḥammad ibn Muḥammad Ghazālī Ṭūsi, Latinized as Algazelus, was a Shafi'i Sunni Muslim Persian scholar
biography: Al-Ghazali, (Persian: ابو حامد محمد ابن محمد غزالی توسی, romanized: Abū Ḥāmid Muḥammad ibn Muḥammad Ghazālī Ṭūsi, Latinized as Algazelus, was a Shafi'i Sunni Muslim Persian scholar and polymath. He is known as one of the most prominent and influential jurisconsults, legal theoreticians, muftis, philosophers, theologians, logicians and mystics in Islamic history.

teachers.name: Alak Zenkar Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Alak Zenkar Rinpoche (Thupten Nyima, b. 1943) is a renowned Nyingma scholar and lineage holder recognized as an emanation of Do Khyentse Yeshe Dorje, known for preserving rare Tibetan texts and teaching Dzogchen.
biography: Alak Zenkar Rinpoche Thupten Nyima was born in 1943 in Lhagang, Minyak, eastern Tibet, and recognized as the incarnation of Alak Zenkar Pema Ngödrup Rolwé Dorje, in the line of Do Khyentse Yeshe Dorje. His principal teachers included the Sixth Dzogchen Rinpoche Jikdral Changchub Dorje and Khenpo Yönten Gönpo.

A leading Tibetan lexicographer and scholar, Rinpoche founded the Project for Searching and Making Available Rare Texts in Chengdu and has served as a visiting scholar at Columbia University and the University of Virginia. He has authored biographies of Jamyang Khyentse Wangpo, Jamgön Kongtrul, and Patrul Rinpoche, and his translations and oral teachings are published through Lotsawa House.

teachers.name: Alexis Nonduality
teachers.shortBio: Alexis Nonduality is a spiritual teacher whose work focuses on non-dual awareness, inner exploration, and guiding people toward realizing fundamental reality.
biography: Alexis Nonduality shares teachings through video and dialogue, pointing to non-dual awareness and the direct recognition of fundamental reality.
The work emphasizes inner exploration and the dissolution of the sense of a separate seeker through clear, accessible pointing.

teachers.name: Ali Hujwiri
teachers.shortBio: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Uthman al-Jullabi al-Hujwiri, known reverentially as Data Sahib, was an Islamic scholar and mystic who authored Kashf al-Mahjub, the earliest treatise on Sufis
biography: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Uthman al-Jullabi al-Hujwiri, known reverentially as Data Sahib, was an Islamic scholar and mystic who authored Kashf al-Mahjub, the earliest treatise on Sufism in the Persian language. Born in the Ghaznavid Empire, al-Hujwiri is believed to have contributed "significantly" to the spread of Islam in South Asia through his preaching.

teachers.name: Anagarika Govinda
teachers.shortBio: Anagarika Govinda was the founder of the order of the Arya Maitreya Mandala and an expositor of Tibetan Buddhism, Abhidharma, and Buddhist meditation as well as other aspects of Bu
biography: Anagarika Govinda was the founder of the order of the Arya Maitreya Mandala and an expositor of Tibetan Buddhism, Abhidharma, and Buddhist meditation as well as other aspects of Buddhism. He was also a painter and poet.

teachers.name: Angelo Dilullo
teachers.shortBio: Angelo Dilullo is a spiritual teacher who shares a clear, accessible approach to awakening and non-duality.
biography: Angelo Dilullo, also known as Dr. Angelo DiLullo, offers a perceptual approach to non-duality that makes awakening accessible through direct experience.
His teaching emphasizes recognizing awareness through everyday perception rather than through complex philosophy or prolonged retreat practice.

biography: Anna Brown is a spiritual teacher and content creator who explores non-dual awareness, consciousness, love, and identity.
Her accessible style invites viewers and students into direct inquiry and the recognition of awareness beyond personal story.

teachers.name: Anthony Bloom
teachers.shortBio: Anthony of Sourozh was best known as a writer and broadcaster on prayer and the Christian life. He was a monk and bishop of the Russian Orthodox Church. He was founder and for many
biography: Anthony of Sourozh was best known as a writer and broadcaster on prayer and the Christian life. He was a monk and bishop of the Russian Orthodox Church. He was founder and for many years bishop - then archbishop, then metropolitan - of the Diocese of Sourozh, the Patriarchate of Moscow's diocese for Great Britain and Ireland. As a bishop he became well known as a pastor, preacher, spiritual director and writer on prayer and the Christian life.

teachers.name: Anyen Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Anyen Rinpoche is a Nyingma master in the Longchen Nyingthig lineage who teaches Dzogchen and Mahamudra internationally, emphasizing practical integration of meditation in daily life.
biography: Anyen Rinpoche was born in Amdo, Tibet, and recognized as a tulku in the Longchen Nyingthig tradition. He received extensive training in sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen under senior Nyingma masters before coming to the West.

He is the author of several books on Dzogchen and meditation and teaches worldwide through Orgyen Khamdroling and affiliated centers, offering guidance on ngöndro, trekchö, tögal, and the union of wisdom and compassion.

teachers.name: Attar of Nishapur
teachers.shortBio: Faridoddin Abu Hamed Mohammad Attar Nishapuri, better known by his pen-names Faridoddin (فریدالدین) and Attar of Nishapur (عطار نیشاپوری, or simply ATTAR,, was a Persian poet, theo
biography: Faridoddin Abu Hamed Mohammad Attar Nishapuri, better known by his pen-names Faridoddin (فریدالدین) and Attar of Nishapur (عطار نیشاپوری, or simply ATTAR,, was a Persian poet, theoretician of Sufism, and hagiographer from Nishapur who had an immense and lasting influence on Persian poetry, on poet Rumi, and on Sufism. He wrote a collection of lyrical poems and number of long poems in the philosophical tradition of Islamic mysticism, as well as a prose work with biographies and sayings of famous Muslim mystics. The Conference of the Birds, Book of the Divine, and Memorial of the Saints are among his best known works.

teachers.name: Augustine of Hippo
teachers.shortBio: Augustine of Hippo was a Christian theologian and philosopher from Thagaste, Numidia Cirtensis and the Bishop of Hippo Regius. He is generally regarded as one of the most influenti
biography: Augustine of Hippo was a Christian theologian and philosopher from Thagaste, Numidia Cirtensis and the Bishop of Hippo Regius. He is generally regarded as one of the most influential philosophers in the history of the Western world, and he is viewed as one of the most important Church Fathers of the Latin Church in the Patristic Period.

teachers.name: Ayya Khema
teachers.shortBio: Ayya Khema was a Buddhist teacher noted for providing opportunities for women to practice Buddhism, founding several centers around the world. In 1987, she helped coordinate the fi
biography: Ayya Khema was a Buddhist teacher noted for providing opportunities for women to practice Buddhism, founding several centers around the world. In 1987, she helped coordinate the first-ever Sakyadhita International Association of Buddhist Women. Over two dozen books of her transcribed Dhamma talks in English and German have been published. In the last year of her life, she also published her autobiography: I Give You My Life.

teachers.name: B. Alan Wallace
teachers.shortBio: Bruce Alan Wallace is an American Buddhologist of Tibetan Buddhism. He has authored many texts in the field of contemplative science, most notably The Attention Revolution on the c
biography: Bruce Alan Wallace is an American Buddhologist of Tibetan Buddhism. He has authored many texts in the field of contemplative science, most notably The Attention Revolution on the cultivation of Samatha, and Dreaming Yourself Awake on the lucid dreaming practice of dream yoga. Wallace's works include an English translation of the foremost Tibetan Buddhism text, the Bardo Thodol, by Padmasambhava. He has also been active in the dialogue between established Western science and Tibetan Buddhist psychology, including staunch critiques of materialistic philosophies of mind, while emphasizing the incorporation of introspection as a technique of academic inquiry. He is founder of the Santa Barbara Institute for Consciousness Studies. Alan Wallace is widely regarded as a preeminent meditation teacher and Buddhism scholar.

teachers.name: Bardor Tulku Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: The Third Bardor Tulku Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist teacher, a holder of the religious lineage of Terchen Barway Dorje. Rinpoche is the founder of a Tibetan Buddhist center, Kun
biography: The Third Bardor Tulku Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist teacher, a holder of the religious lineage of Terchen Barway Dorje. Rinpoche is the founder of a Tibetan Buddhist center, Kunzang Palchen Ling, and the Raktrul Foundation, in Red Hook, New York.

teachers.shortBio: Muhammad Raheem Bawa Muhaiyaddeen, also known as Bawa, was a Tamil-speaking teacher and Sufi mystic from Sri Lanka who came to the United States in 1971, established a following, a
biography: Muhammad Raheem Bawa Muhaiyaddeen, also known as Bawa, was a Tamil-speaking teacher and Sufi mystic from Sri Lanka who came to the United States in 1971, established a following, and founded the Bawa Muhaiyaddeen Fellowship in Philadelphia. He developed branches in the United States, Canada, Australia and the UK — adding to existing groups in Jaffna and Colombo, Sri Lanka. He is known for his teachings, discourses, songs, and artwork.

biography: Bāyazīd Basṭāmī,, commonly known as the Sultān-ul-Ārifīn was a Persian Sufi who became famous for his bold articulation of fanāʾ–the Sufi notion of self-annhilation.

teachers.shortBio: Bede Griffiths OSB Cam, born Alan Richard Griffiths and also known by the end of his life as Swami Dayananda, was a British Catholic priest and Benedictine monk who lived in ashram
biography: Bede Griffiths OSB Cam, born Alan Richard Griffiths and also known by the end of his life as Swami Dayananda, was a British Catholic priest and Benedictine monk who lived in ashrams in South India and became a noted missionary. Griffiths was a part of the Christian Ashram Movement.

teachers.name: Bernie Glassman
teachers.shortBio: Bernie Glassman was an American Zen Buddhist roshi, aeronautical engineer and founder of the Zen Peacemakers, an organization established in 1980. In 1996, he co-founded the Zen Pe
biography: Bernie Glassman was an American Zen Buddhist roshi, aeronautical engineer and founder of the Zen Peacemakers, an organization established in 1980. In 1996, he co-founded the Zen Peacemaker Order with his late wife, Sandra Jishu Holmes. Glassman was a Dharma heir of the late Taizan Maezumi-roshi, and gave inka and Dharma transmission to several people.

biography: Beth Upton is a meditation teacher and former Buddhist nun who offers personalized guidance for deepening practice.
Her teaching draws on the Theravāda insight tradition, supporting students through one-to-one mentorship and embodied mindfulness.

biography: Nityananda was an Indian guru. His teachings are published in the "Chidakash Gita". Nityananda was born in Koyilandy (Pandalayini), Madras Presidency, British India.

teachers.shortBio: Bhante Henepola Gunaratana is a Sri Lankan Theravada Buddhist monk. He is affectionately known as Bhante G. Bhante Gunaratana is currently the abbot of the Bhavana Society, a monas
biography: Bhante Henepola Gunaratana is a Sri Lankan Theravada Buddhist monk. He is affectionately known as Bhante G. Bhante Gunaratana is currently the abbot of the Bhavana Society, a monastery and meditation retreat center that he founded in High View, West Virginia, in 1985. He is the author of the best-selling meditation guide Mindfulness in Plain English (1992).

teachers.name: Bhikkhu Bodhi
teachers.shortBio: Jeffrey Block, better known as Bhikkhu Bodhi, is an American Theravada monk ordained in Sri Lanka who teaches in the area of New York and New Jersey. He is an author and Buddhist c
biography: Jeffrey Block, better known as Bhikkhu Bodhi, is an American Theravada monk ordained in Sri Lanka who teaches in the area of New York and New Jersey. He is an author and Buddhist commentator and was appointed the second president of the Buddhist Publication Society. He is also the president of the Buddhist Association of America and the founder of Buddhist Global Relief.

biography: Heȟáka Sápa, commonly known as Black Elk, was a wičháša wakȟáŋ and heyoka of the Oglala Lakota people. He was a second cousin of the war leader Crazy Horse and fought with him in the Battle of Little Bighorn. He survived the Wounded Knee Massacre in 1890. He toured and performed in Europe as part of Buffalo Bill's Wild West.

teachers.name: Bodhidharma
teachers.shortBio: Bodhidharma  was a semi-legendary Buddhist monk who lived during the 5th or 6th century CE. He is traditionally credited as the transmitter of Chan Buddhism to China, and is regard
biography: Bodhidharma  was a semi-legendary Buddhist monk who lived during the 5th or 6th century CE. He is traditionally credited as the transmitter of Chan Buddhism to China, and is regarded as its first Chinese patriarch. He is also popularly regarded as the founder of Shaolin kung fu, an idea popularized in the 20th century, but based on the 17th century Yijin Jing and the Daoist association of daoyin gymnastics with Bodhidharma.

biography: Richard Francis Xavier Manning, known as Brennan Manning, was an American author, laicized Catholic priest, and public speaker. He is best known for his bestselling book The Ragamuffin Gospel.

teachers.shortBio: Brother Lawrence of the Resurrection, OCD was a French Catholic religious brother who served at a Discalced Carmelite monastery, what is now Saint-Joseph-des-Carmes in Paris. He is
biography: Brother Lawrence of the Resurrection, OCD was a French Catholic religious brother who served at a Discalced Carmelite monastery, what is now Saint-Joseph-des-Carmes in Paris. He is best remembered for a posthumous book of his writing, the classic Christian text The Practice of the Presence of God.

teachers.name: Brother Roger
teachers.shortBio: Roger Schutz, popularly known as Brother Roger, was a Swiss Christian leader and monastic brother. In 1940 Schutz founded the Taizé Community, an ecumenical monastic community in B
biography: Roger Schutz, popularly known as Brother Roger, was a Swiss Christian leader and monastic brother. In 1940 Schutz founded the Taizé Community, an ecumenical monastic community in Burgundy, France, serving as its first prior until his murder in 2005. Towards the end of his life, the Taizé Community was attracting international attention, welcoming thousands of young pilgrims every week, which it has continued to do after his death.

teachers.name: Buddhadasa
teachers.shortBio: Buddhadasa was a Thai Buddhist monk. Known as an innovative reinterpreter of Buddhist doctrine and Thai folk beliefs, he fostered a reformation in conventional religious perception
biography: Buddhadasa was a Thai Buddhist monk. Known as an innovative reinterpreter of Buddhist doctrine and Thai folk beliefs, he fostered a reformation in conventional religious perceptions in his home country, Thailand, as well as abroad. He developed a personal view that those who have penetrated the essential nature of religions consider "all religions to be inwardly the same", while those who have the highest understanding of dhamma feel "there is no religion". Buddhadasa was also known for his political engagement and developed a form of Buddhist socialism he called "Dhammic socialism".

teachers.name: Bulleh Shah
teachers.shortBio: Sayyid Abdullāh Shāh Qādrī, popularly known as Baba Bulleh Shah and vocatively as Bulleya, was a Punjabi revolutionary philosopher, reformer and poet, regarded as one of the greate
biography: Sayyid Abdullāh Shāh Qādrī, popularly known as Baba Bulleh Shah and vocatively as Bulleya, was a Punjabi revolutionary philosopher, reformer and poet, regarded as one of the greatest poets of the Punjabi language and revered as the 'Father of Punjabi Enlightenment'. His literary oeuvre, comprising ~150 kafi and 94 other poetic compositions, reflect themes of Sufi and humanist philosophy — for which he is regarded as 'Poet of the People' among Punjabis.

teachers.name: Byron Katie
teachers.shortBio: Byron Kathleen Mitchell, better known as Byron Katie, is an American speaker and author who teaches a method of self-inquiry known as "The Work of Byron Katie" or simply as "The Wo
biography: Byron Kathleen Mitchell, better known as Byron Katie, is an American speaker and author who teaches a method of self-inquiry known as "The Work of Byron Katie" or simply as "The Work". She is the founder of Byron Katie International, an organization that includes the School for the Work and Turnaround House in Ojai, California. Time Magazine describes her as "a spiritual innovator for the 21st century."

bibliography.title: A mind at home with itself

teachers.name: Catherine of Siena
teachers.shortBio: Caterina di Jacopo di Benincasa, known as Catherine of Siena, was an Italian Catholic mystic and diplomat who engaged in papal and Italian politics through extensive letter-writing
biography: Caterina di Jacopo di Benincasa, known as Catherine of Siena, was an Italian Catholic mystic and diplomat who engaged in papal and Italian politics through extensive letter-writing and advocacy. Canonized in 1461, she is revered as a saint and as a Doctor of the Church due to her extensive theological authorship. She is also considered to have influenced Italian literature.

Dialogue of St. Catherine of Siena

teachers.name: Chamtrul Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Chamtrul Rinpoche Lobsang Gyatso is a Nyingma khenpo and recognized incarnation of the Kathok Chamtrul line who teaches Dzogchen, ngöndro, and Buddhist philosophy worldwide.
biography: Chamtrul Rinpoche Lobsang Gyatso was born in Golok, eastern Tibet, and recognized as the reincarnation of the second Kathok Chamtrul Pema Nangsel Dorje of Mardo Tashi Choling Monastery. He studied under Dzogchen master Naljor Yeshe Wangchuk and completed the five sciences at Kathok Monastery and Larung Gar, earning the title of khenpo.

Since 1999 he has been based in Dharamsala, where he directs the Bodhicitta Dharma Centre. He travels extensively teaching ngöndro, Dzogchen, and tantric generation and completion stage practices to students in more than a hundred countries.

teachers.name: Chaphur Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Chaphur Rinpoche is a Nyingma master and tertön in the Golok region, holder of the Chaphur lineage and a principal teacher of Dzogchen in eastern Tibet and internationally.
biography: Chaphur Rinpoche was recognized as the incarnation of the Chaphur lineage of tertöns in Golok, eastern Tibet. He received the complete Nyingma transmissions, including the Longchen Nyingthig and his family lineage treasures, from senior masters of the region.
He established Dharma centers and supports monastic and lay communities in Tibet while teaching Dzogchen and treasure teachings to students in Asia and the West.

teachers.name: Charlotte Joko Beck
teachers.shortBio: Charlotte Joko Beck was an American Zen teacher and the author of the books Everyday Zen: Love and Work and Nothing Special: Living Zen.
biography: Charlotte Joko Beck was an American Zen teacher and the author of the books Everyday Zen: Love and Work and Nothing Special: Living Zen.

teachers.name: Chinmayananda Saraswati
teachers.shortBio: Swami Chinmayananda Saraswati, also known as Pujya Gurudev Swami Chinmayananda Saraswati, was a Hindu spiritual leader and a teacher. In 1953, he founded Chinmaya Mission, a worldw
biography: Swami Chinmayananda Saraswati, also known as Pujya Gurudev Swami Chinmayananda Saraswati, was a Hindu spiritual leader and a teacher. In 1953, he founded Chinmaya Mission, a worldwide nonprofit organisation, in order to spread the knowledge of Advaita Vedanta, the Bhagavad Gita, the Upanishads, and other ancient Hindu scriptures. Through the Mission, Swami Chinmayananda spearheaded a global Hindu spiritual and cultural renaissance that popularised these spiritual texts and values, teaching them in English all across India and abroad.

bibliography.title: Discourses on Māṇḍukya Upaniṣad with Gauḍapāda's Kārikā

teachers.name: Chögyal Namkhai Norbu
teachers.shortBio: Namkhai Norbu was a Tibetan Buddhist master of Dzogchen and a professor of Tibetan and Mongolian language and literature at Naples Eastern University. He was a leading authority on
biography: Namkhai Norbu was a Tibetan Buddhist master of Dzogchen and a professor of Tibetan and Mongolian language and literature at Naples Eastern University. He was a leading authority on Tibetan culture, particularly in the fields of history, literature, traditional religions, and Traditional Tibetan medicine, having written numerous books and scholarly articles on these subjects.

teachers.name: Chögyam Trungpa
teachers.shortBio: Chögyam Trungpa, formally named the 11th Zurmang Trungpa, Chokyi Gyatso, was a Tibetan Buddhist master and holder of both Kagyu and Nyingma lineages of Tibetan Buddhism. He was rec
biography: Chögyam Trungpa, formally named the 11th Zurmang Trungpa, Chokyi Gyatso, was a Tibetan Buddhist master and holder of both Kagyu and Nyingma lineages of Tibetan Buddhism. He was recognized by both Tibetan Buddhists and other spiritual practitioners and scholars as a preeminent teacher of Tibetan Buddhism. He was a major figure in the dissemination of Buddhism in the West, founding Vajradhatu and Naropa University and establishing the Shambhala Training method. The 11th of the Trungpa tülkus, he was a tertön, supreme abbot of the Surmang monasteries, scholar, teacher, poet, artist, and originator of Shambhala Buddhist tradition.

teachers.name: Shyé Chen Kong Trul Pema Drimé
Chokling Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tsikey Chokling Rinpoche is the fourth incarnation in the Chokling lineage, a principal lineage holder of the Chokling Tersar treasure revelations of his predecessor Chokgyur Lingpa.
biography: Tsikey Chokling Rinpoche was recognized as the fourth incarnation of Chokgyur Dechen Lingpa's son and lineage holder. He received the complete transmissions of the Chokling Tersar and Nyingma kama from his father, Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche, and other masters.
He oversees monastic and retreat centers in Nepal and teaches the treasure cycles of Chokgyur Lingpa, including Mahamudra and Dzogchen instructions, to students worldwide.

teachers.name: Chökyi Nyima Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Chökyi Nyima Rinpoche is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher and meditation master. He is the abbot of Ka-Nying Shedrub Ling Monastery in Kathmandu, Nepal. He is the author of several books
biography: Chökyi Nyima Rinpoche is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher and meditation master. He is the abbot of Ka-Nying Shedrub Ling Monastery in Kathmandu, Nepal. He is the author of several books, founder of meditation centers around the world, and an international teacher.

teachers.name: Chongtul Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Chongtul Rinpoche is a Nyingma tulku and Dzogchen teacher in the Golok tradition, recognized for preserving and transmitting the pith instructions of the Great Perfection.
biography: Chongtul Rinpoche was recognized as a tulku in the Nyingma tradition of Golok, eastern Tibet. He completed the traditional curriculum of sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen under senior lineage masters.
He teaches Dzogchen and supports monastic communities, offering the direct instructions of trekchö and tögal to qualified students in Tibet and abroad.

teachers.shortBio: Christiane Michelberger is a spiritual guide who helps seekers awaken by seeing through the illusion of a separate self, drawing from her own direct experience.
biography: Christiane Michelberger guides seekers through a path-based approach rooted in her own direct experience of awakening.
Her work focuses on seeing through the illusion of a separate self through inquiry, presence, and embodied understanding.

teachers.name: Credo Mutwa
teachers.shortBio: Vusamazulu Credo Mutwa was a Zulu sangoma from South Africa. He was known as an author of books that draw upon African mythology, traditional Zulu folklore, extraterrestrial encoun
biography: Vusamazulu Credo Mutwa was a Zulu sangoma from South Africa. He was known as an author of books that draw upon African mythology, traditional Zulu folklore, extraterrestrial encounters and his own personal encounters. His last work was a graphic novel called the Tree of Life Trilogy based on his writings of his most famous book, Indaba my Children. In 2018 he was honoured with an USIBA award presented by the South African Department of Arts and Culture, for his work in indigenous wisdom.

teachers.name: D. T. Suzuki
teachers.shortBio: Daisetz Teitaro Suzuki , was a Japanese essayist, philosopher, religious scholar, and translator. He was an authority on Buddhism, especially Zen and Shin, and was instrumental in
biography: Daisetz Teitaro Suzuki , was a Japanese essayist, philosopher, religious scholar, and translator. He was an authority on Buddhism, especially Zen and Shin, and was instrumental in spreading interest in these to the West. He was also a prolific translator of Chinese, Korean, Japanese, Vietnamese and Sanskrit literature. Suzuki spent several lengthy stretches teaching or lecturing at Western universities and devoted many years to a professorship at Ōtani University, a Japanese university of the Ōtani School of Jōdo Shinshū.

teachers.name: Dainin Katagiri
teachers.shortBio: Jikai Dainin Katagiri , was a Sōtō Zen priest and teacher, and the founding abbot of Minnesota Zen Meditation Center in Minneapolis, Minnesota, where he served from 1972 until his
biography: Jikai Dainin Katagiri , was a Sōtō Zen priest and teacher, and the founding abbot of Minnesota Zen Meditation Center in Minneapolis, Minnesota, where he served from 1972 until his death from cancer in 1990. He is also the founder of Hokyoji Zen Practice Community in Eitzen, Minnesota. Before becoming first abbot of the Minnesota Zen Meditation Center, Katagiri had worked at the Zenshuji Soto Zen Mission in Los Angeles and had also been of great service to Shunryu Suzuki at the San Francisco Zen Center, particularly from 1969 until Suzuki's death in 1971. Katagiri was important in helping bring Zen Buddhism from Japan to the United States during its formative years. He is also the credited author of several books compiled from his talks.

teachers.name: Daniel Ingram
teachers.shortBio: Daniel M. Ingram is a meditation author and researcher best known for Mastering the Core Teachings of the Buddha, presenting a detailed experiential map of insight and enlightenment practice.
biography: Daniel M. Ingram is an American meditation author, researcher, and former emergency-medicine physician best known for Mastering the Core Teachings of the Buddha.
His pragmatic dharma approach offers a detailed, experiential map of insight stages and enlightenment practice grounded in rigorous first-person investigation.

teachers.name: Davi Kopenawa
teachers.shortBio: Davi Kopenawa Yanomami, name also written Davi Kobenawä Yanomamö, is a Yanomami shaman and Portuguese-speaking spokesperson for the Yanomami people in Brazil. He became known for h
biography: Davi Kopenawa Yanomami, name also written Davi Kobenawä Yanomamö, is a Yanomami shaman and Portuguese-speaking spokesperson for the Yanomami people in Brazil. He became known for his advocacy regarding tribal issues and Amazon rainforest conservation when the tribal rights organization Survival International invited him to accept the Right Livelihood Award on its behalf in 1989. Since then, Kopenawa has traveled around the globe to speak on the catastrophic consequences of the invasion of Yanomami land and the destruction of the Amazon rainforest. As part of his advocacy for the Yanomami people and against extractivist exploitation, Kopenawa has co-authored and contributed to multiple published books that have been translated to multiple languages.

teachers.name: Dayananda Saraswati
teachers.shortBio: Swami Dayananda Saraswati was a renunciate monk of the Hindu Saraswati order of sannyasa. He was also known as Pujya Swamiji and was a traditional teacher of Advaita Vedanta. He wa
biography: Swami Dayananda Saraswati was a renunciate monk of the Hindu Saraswati order of sannyasa. He was also known as Pujya Swamiji and was a traditional teacher of Advaita Vedanta. He was the founder of the Arsha Vidya Gurukulams in Pennsylvania, USA; Rishikesh, Uttarakhand and Coimbatore Tamil Nadu, India. He was also the spiritual Guru of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He was awarded the Padma Bhushan,, for his service to the nation in the field of spirituality in 2016.

teachers.name: Dilgo Khyentse
teachers.shortBio: Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche, Tashi Paljor was a Vajrayana master, Terton, scholar, poet, teacher, and recognized by Buddhists as one of the greatest realized masters. Head of the Nying
biography: Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche, Tashi Paljor was a Vajrayana master, Terton, scholar, poet, teacher, and recognized by Buddhists as one of the greatest realized masters. Head of the Nyingma school of Tibetan Buddhism from 1988 to 1991, he is also considered an eminent proponent of the Rime tradition.

teachers.name: Dipa Ma

biography: Nani Bala Barua, better known as Dipa Ma, was an Indian meditation teacher of Theravada Buddhism and was of Barua descent. She was a prominent Buddhist master in Asia and also taught in the United States where she influenced the American branch of the Vipassana movement.

teachers.name: Dodrubchen Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Dodrubchen Rinpoche Jigme Trinle Palbar is the seventh head of Dodrupchen Monastery and a principal holder of the Longchen Nyingthig Dzogchen lineage.
biography: The Seventh Dodrubchen Rinpoche, Jigme Trinle Palbar, was recognized as the incarnation of the great Dzogchen master Dodrubchen Jigme Tenpai Nyima. He received the complete Nyingma transmissions from Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche, Penor Rinpoche, and other masters.

As head of Dodrupchen Monastery in Gangtok and Dehradun, he preserves and teaches the Longchen Nyingthig cycle, including the Dzogchen instructions of trekchö and tögal, to monastics and lay practitioners worldwide.

teachers.name: Dōgen
teachers.shortBio: Dōgen Zenji  was a Japanese Zen Buddhist monk, writer, poet, philosopher, and founder of the Sōtō school of Zen in Japan. He is also known as Dōgen Kigen (道元希玄), Eihei Dōgen (永平道元)
biography: Dōgen Zenji  was a Japanese Zen Buddhist monk, writer, poet, philosopher, and founder of the Sōtō school of Zen in Japan. He is also known as Dōgen Kigen (道元希玄), Eihei Dōgen (永平道元), Kōso Jōyō Daishi (高祖承陽大師), and Busshō Dentō Kokushi (仏性伝東国師).

teachers.name: Douglas Harding
teachers.shortBio: Douglas Edison Harding was an English philosophical writer, mystic, spiritual teacher. He authored several books, including On Having No Head: Zen and the Rediscovery of the Obviou
biography: Douglas Edison Harding was an English philosophical writer, mystic, spiritual teacher. He authored several books, including On Having No Head: Zen and the Rediscovery of the Obvious (1961), which presents practical methods aimed at helping readers directly experience non-duality and the concept of anattā (selflessness), rather than merely understanding them intellectually.

teachers.name: Drimed Lodro Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Lama Drimed Lodro Rinpoche is a Nyingma lama trained at Larung Gar who teaches Dzogchen and the Nyingtik cycles at Orgyen Dorje Den in California.
biography: Lama Drimed Lodro Rinpoche was born in Tibet and entered Sertha Larung Five Sciences Buddhist Academy at fifteen, completing fourteen years of training toward khenpo certification. He received the Nyingtik Yabzhi, Dzod Dun, Yeshe Lama, and other Dzogchen transmissions from H.H. Jigme Tromge Rinpoche, H.H. Penor Rinpoche, and Ven. Yangthang Rinpoche.

At the request of his uncle Ven. Gyatrul Rinpoche, he began teaching in the United States in 2003. He founded Shensem Tsogpa Tibetan Cultural Center and Dechen Rang Dharma Center, and currently resides at Orgyen Dorje Den in Alameda, where he gives Vajrayana and Dzogchen teachings.

teachers.name: Dungse Rigzin Dorje Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Dungse Rigzin Dorje Rinpoche is the son and lineage holder of H.E. Chagdud Tulku Rinpoche, teaching Dzogchen and the Namchö cycle internationally.
biography: Dungse Rigzin Dorje Rinpoche is the son of H.E. Chagdud Tulku Rinpoche and was recognized and trained in the Nyingma tradition. He received the Namchö and Dzogchen transmissions from his father and other masters.
He teaches through Chagdud Gonpa centers in North and South America, offering ngöndro, Phowa, and Dzogchen instructions from the Namchö treasure lineage.

teachers.name: Dzigar Kongtrul Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Dzigar Kongtrul Rinpoche is the title of a tulku lineage of Tibetan Buddhist lamas. They originate with Jamgon Kongtrul Lodro Thaye, one of the most illustrious lamas of recent his
biography: Dzigar Kongtrul Rinpoche is the title of a tulku lineage of Tibetan Buddhist lamas. They originate with Jamgon Kongtrul Lodro Thaye, one of the most illustrious lamas of recent history, known for his central role in the rimé or non-sectarian movement in 19th Century Tibet. Jigme Namgyel is the present Dzigar Kongtrul Rinpoche. He is the second or third incarnation, depending on whether Lodro Thaye is counted.

teachers.name: Dzogchen Ponlop Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: The 7th Dzogchen Ponlop is an abbot of Dzogchen Monastery, founder and spiritual director of Nalandabodhi, founder of Nītārtha Institute for Higher Buddhist Studies, a leading Tibe
biography: The 7th Dzogchen Ponlop is an abbot of Dzogchen Monastery, founder and spiritual director of Nalandabodhi, founder of Nītārtha Institute for Higher Buddhist Studies, a leading Tibetan Buddhist scholar, and a meditation master. He is one of the highest tülkus in the Nyingma lineage and an accomplished Karma Kagyu lineage holder.

teachers.name: Dzongsar Jamyang Khyentse
teachers.shortBio: Dzongsar Jamyang Khyentse Rinpoche, also known as Khyentse Norbu, is a Tibetan Lama, filmmaker, and writer who was born in Bhutan. His five major films are The Cup (1999), Travelle
biography: Dzongsar Jamyang Khyentse Rinpoche, also known as Khyentse Norbu, is a Tibetan Lama, filmmaker, and writer who was born in Bhutan. His five major films are The Cup (1999), Travellers and Magicians (2003), Vara: A Blessing (2013), Hema Hema: Sing Me a Song While I Wait (2017), and Looking for a Lady with Fangs and a Moustache (2019). He is the author of several published books such as What Makes You Not a Buddhist (2007), Not For Happiness: A Guide to the So-Called Preliminary Practices (2012), The Guru Drinks Bourbon? (2016), Living is Dying (2020), and several non-fiction works on Tibetan Buddhism for free distribution such as Introduction to the Middle Way: Chandrakirti’s Madhyamaka with Commentary (2003) and Buddha Nature: Mahayana-Uttaratantra-Shastra with Commentary (2007). He has also written an autobiography in process entitled Mugwort Born. Many of his teachings are available on the Siddhartha's Intent YouTube channel.

teachers.name: Eckhart Tolle
teachers.shortBio: Eckhart Tolle is a German “spiritual teacher” and self-help author. His books include The Power of Now: A Guide to Spiritual Enlightenment (1997), A New Earth: Awakening to Your Li
biography: Eckhart Tolle is a German “spiritual teacher” and self-help author. His books include The Power of Now: A Guide to Spiritual Enlightenment (1997), A New Earth: Awakening to Your Life's Purpose (2005) and the picture book Guardians of Being (2009).

teachers.name: Elena Nezhinsky
teachers.shortBio: Elena Nezhinsky is a spiritual teacher and co-founder of Liberation Unleashed who guides seekers through direct inquiry to see through the illusion of a separate self.
biography: Elena Nezhinsky is a co-founder of Liberation Unleashed and a guide in the tradition of direct no-self inquiry.
She helps seekers look plainly at experience until the sense of a separate self is seen through and the simplicity of being is recognized.

teachers.name: Elias Capriles
teachers.shortBio: Elias Capriles is a Venezuelan philosopher and Buddhist scholar who has written extensively on Dzogchen, Madhyamaka, and the intersection of Buddhism with contemporary thought.
biography: Elias Capriles is a philosopher, author, and long-time student of Tibetan Buddhism with a focus on Dzogchen and Madhyamaka. His academic and practice-oriented writings explore the Great Perfection in dialogue with Western philosophy.
He has published numerous books and articles on Buddhist epistemology, Dzogchen, and the path of awakening, contributing to the scholarly understanding of Tibetan contemplative traditions.

teachers.name: Emerson Nonduality
teachers.shortBio: Emerson Nonduality is a modern spiritual teacher who shares a direct, experiential approach to awakening, guiding people beyond the illusion of a separate self.
biography: Emerson Nonduality offers a direct, experiential approach to awakening that points beyond the illusion of a separate self.
The teaching is contemporary and accessible, emphasizing immediate recognition rather than progressive spiritual attainment.

teachers.name: Erik Pema Kunsang
teachers.shortBio: Erik Pema Kunsang is a Danish Dharma teacher and translator. He was, along with Marcia Binder Schmidt, director of Rangjung Yeshe Translations and Publications in Kathmandu. He has
biography: Erik Pema Kunsang is a Danish Dharma teacher and translator. He was, along with Marcia Binder Schmidt, director of Rangjung Yeshe Translations and Publications in Kathmandu. He has translated over fifty volumes of Tibetan texts and oral teachings. Erik has been the assistant and translator for Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche and his sons since the late 1970s. He was active in facilitating masters of the Practice Lineages to teach in the West.

teachers.name: Eshwar Segobind
teachers.shortBio: Eshwar Segobind is a spiritual teacher who guides individuals to recognize the illusion of a separate self and directly experience the inherent freedom and clarity of being.
biography: Eshwar Segobind guides seekers to recognize the illusion of a separate self and rest in the inherent freedom and clarity of being.
His teaching is awareness-based, pointing to direct experience rather than belief or progressive practice.

teachers.shortBio: Evelyn Underhill was an English Anglo-Catholic writer and pacifist known for her numerous works on religion and spiritual practice, in particular Christian mysticism. Her best-know
biography: Evelyn Underhill was an English Anglo-Catholic writer and pacifist known for her numerous works on religion and spiritual practice, in particular Christian mysticism. Her best-known work is Mysticism, published in 1911.

teachers.name: Francis of Assisi
teachers.shortBio: Giovanni di Pietro di Bernardone, known as Francis of Assisi, was an Italian mystic, poet and Catholic friar who founded the religious order of the Franciscans. Inspired to lead a
biography: Giovanni di Pietro di Bernardone, known as Francis of Assisi, was an Italian mystic, poet and Catholic friar who founded the religious order of the Franciscans. Inspired to lead a Christian life of poverty, he became a beggar and an itinerant preacher.

teachers.name: Frank Fools Crow
teachers.shortBio: Frank Fools Crow was an Oglala Lakota civic and religious leader. 'Grandfather', or 'Grandpa Frank' as he was often called, was a nephew of Black Elk who worked to preserve Lakota
biography: Frank Fools Crow was an Oglala Lakota civic and religious leader. 'Grandfather', or 'Grandpa Frank' as he was often called, was a nephew of Black Elk who worked to preserve Lakota traditions, including the Sun Dance and yuwipi ceremonies. He supported Lakota sovereignty and treaty rights, and was a leader of the traditional faction during the armed standoff at Wounded Knee in 1973. With writer Thomas E. Mails, he produced two books about his life and work, Fools Crow in 1979, and Fools Crow: Wisdom and Power in 1990.

teachers.name: Frank Yang
teachers.shortBio: Frank Yang is a contemporary non-duality teacher who emphasizes direct, experiential realization of awareness, challenging conventional beliefs about self, identity, and personal transformation.
biography: Frank Yang is a contemporary non-duality teacher who emphasizes direct, experiential realization of awareness.
His teaching challenges conventional beliefs about self, identity, and personal transformation, inviting students into immediate recognition rather than gradual self-improvement.

biography: Frithjof Schuon was a Swiss philosopher and spiritual leader, belonging to the Traditionalist School of Perennialism. He was the author of more than twenty works in French on metaphysics, spirituality, religion, anthropology, and art. He was also a painter and a poet.

teachers.shortBio: Gangteng Tulku Rinpoche is the ninth Gangteng trulku and head of Gangteng Monastery in Bhutan, a principal holder of the Pema Lingpa treasure lineage.
biography: Gangteng Tulku Rinpoche Kunzang Rigdzin Pema Namgyal was recognized as the ninth incarnation of Pema Lingpa's lineage throne-holder at Gangteng Monastery in the Phobjikha Valley of Bhutan.
He received the complete transmissions of the Pema Lingpa treasures and Nyingma kama, and teaches the treasure cycles—including Dzogchen instructions—throughout Bhutan and internationally.

teachers.name: Garchen Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: The Eighth Garchen Rinpoche, also called Garchen Triptrul Rinpoche, is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher in the Drikung Kagyu lineage. Born April 1936 in Nangchen, Kham, he is believed to
biography: The Eighth Garchen Rinpoche, also called Garchen Triptrul Rinpoche, is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher in the Drikung Kagyu lineage. Born April 1936 in Nangchen, Kham, he is believed to be an incarnation of Siddha Gar Chodingpa, a heart-disciple of Jigten Sumgön, founder of the Drikung Kagyu lineage in the thirteenth century CE. He is also believed to have incarnated as Mahasiddha Aryadeva in ancient India - the lotus-born disciple of Nagarjuna himself. He was known as Lonpo Gar, the minister of Tibetan dharma king Songtsen Gampo in the seventh century CE.

teachers.name: Geshe Dangsong Namgyal
teachers.shortBio: Geshe Dangsong Namgyal is a Bönpo geshe, author, and teacher of Dzogchen and the ancient Zhangzhung spiritual traditions.
biography: Geshe Dangsong Namgyal trained at Menri Monastery in the Bön tradition, completing advanced studies in philosophy, tantra, and Dzogchen. He is the author of several books on Bön meditation and spirituality.
He teaches the Zhangzhung Nyengyud Dzogchen cycle and Bön practices through the Shen Foundation and affiliated centers in the United States and internationally.

teachers.name: Geshe YongDong
teachers.shortBio: Geshe YongDong Sherab Gyaltsen is a Bönpo geshe and founder of Sherab Chamma Ling, teaching Dzogchen and the Zhangzhung Nyengyud in Canada.
biography: Geshe YongDong Sherab Gyaltsen was born in Amdo and trained at Menri Monastery, earning the geshe degree in Bön philosophy, tantra, and Dzogchen.
He founded Sherab Chamma Ling in Courtenay, British Columbia, teaching the Zhangzhung Nyengyud Dzogchen cycle, meditation, and Bön spiritual practices to students in North America and Asia.

teachers.name: Gil Fronsdal
teachers.shortBio: Gil Fronsdal is a Norwegian-born, American Buddhist teacher, writer and scholar based in Redwood City, California. He has been practicing Buddhism of the Sōtō Zen and Vipassanā sec
biography: Gil Fronsdal is a Norwegian-born, American Buddhist teacher, writer and scholar based in Redwood City, California. He has been practicing Buddhism of the Sōtō Zen and Vipassanā sects since 1975, and is currently teaching the practice of Buddhism in the San Francisco Bay Area. Having been taught by the Vipassanā practitioner Jack Kornfield, Fronsdal is part of the Vipassanā teachers' collective at Spirit Rock Meditation Center. He was ordained as a Sōtō Zen priest at the San Francisco Zen Center in 1982, and was a Theravāda monk in Burma in 1985. In 1995, he received Dharma transmission from Mel Weitsman, the abbot of the Berkeley Zen Center.

bibliography.title: The Buddha before Buddhism [Nov 30, 2017] Gil Fronsdal

teachers.name: Gregory of Nyssa
teachers.shortBio: Gregory of Nyssa, also known as Gregory Nyssen, was an early Christian theologian who served as the bishop of Nyssa from 372 to 376 and from 378 until his death in 394. He is vener
biography: Gregory of Nyssa, also known as Gregory Nyssen, was an early Christian theologian who served as the bishop of Nyssa from 372 to 376 and from 378 until his death in 394. He is venerated as a saint in Eastern Orthodoxy, the Catholic Church, Oriental Orthodox Churches, Anglicanism, and Lutheranism. Gregory, his elder brother Basil of Caesarea, and their friend Gregory of Nazianzus are collectively known as the Cappadocian Fathers.

teachers.name: Gregory Palamas
teachers.shortBio: Gregory Palamas was a Byzantine Greek theologian and Eastern Orthodox cleric of the late Byzantine period. A monk of Mount Athos and later archbishop of Thessalonica, he is famous
biography: Gregory Palamas was a Byzantine Greek theologian and Eastern Orthodox cleric of the late Byzantine period. A monk of Mount Athos and later archbishop of Thessalonica, he is famous for his defense of hesychast spirituality, the uncreated character of the light of the Transfiguration, and the distinction between God's essence and energies. His teaching unfolded over the course of three major controversies, (1) with the Italo-Greek Barlaam between 1336 and 1341, (2) with the monk Gregory Akindynos between 1341 and 1347, and (3) with the philosopher Gregoras, from 1348 to 1355. His theological contributions are sometimes referred to as Palamism, and his followers as Palamites.

bibliography.title: The homilies of Saint Gregory Palamas

biography: Gurumayi Chidvilasananda, born Malti Shetty on 24 June 1955, is the guru or spiritual head of the Siddha Yoga path, with ashrams and meditation centers in both India and the Western world.

teachers.name: Gyatrul Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Gyatrul Rinpoche (1930–2023) was a Nyingma master and founder of Tashi Chöling in Oregon, renowned for teaching Dzogchen and the Dudjom Tersar lineage in the West.
biography: Gyatrul Rinpoche was born in what is now Qinghai, China, and recognized as the incarnation of a Palyul monastery lama. He studied at Palyul and Mindrolling monasteries and completed extensive retreat before fleeing Tibet in 1959.

He founded Tashi Chöling in Ashland, Oregon, and taught the Dudjom Tersar, Longchen Nyingthig, and Dzogchen pith instructions to Western students for decades, emphasizing shamatha, ngöndro, and the nature of mind.

teachers.name: H.H. the 34th Menri Trizin
teachers.shortBio: H.H. Lungtok Tenpai Nyima Rinpoche is the 34th Menri Trizin, supreme spiritual head of the Yungdrung Bön tradition and holder of its Dzogchen teachings.
biography: His Holiness the 34th Menri Trizin Lungtok Tenpai Nyima Rinpoche was born in Amdo and recognized as the spiritual head of the Bön tradition. He trained at Menri Monastery under the 33rd Menri Trizin, mastering Bön philosophy, tantra, and Dzogchen.

As Menri Trizin, he leads Menri Monastery in Dolanji, India, and the worldwide Bön community, preserving and transmitting the Zhangzhung Nyengyud and other Dzogchen cycles of the ancient Bön tradition.

teachers.name: Hafez
teachers.shortBio: Khajeh Shams-od-Din Mohammad Hafez Shirazi (1325–1390), also known by his pen name Hafez, was a Persian lyric poet whose collected works are regarded by many Iranians as one of the
biography: Khajeh Shams-od-Din Mohammad Hafez Shirazi (1325–1390), also known by his pen name Hafez, was a Persian lyric poet whose collected works are regarded by many Iranians as one of the highest pinnacles of Persian literature.His works are often found in the homes of Persian speakers, who learn his poems by heart and use them as everyday proverbs and sayings. His life and poems have become the subjects of much analysis, commentary, and interpretation, influencing post-14th century Persian writing more than any other Persian author. He is also known by his sobriquet Lisan al-Ghayb.

teachers.name: Hakuin Ekaku
teachers.shortBio: Hakuin Ekaku  was one of the most influential figures in Japanese Zen Buddhism, who regarded bodhicitta, working for the benefit of others, as the ultimate concern of Zen-training.
biography: Hakuin Ekaku  was one of the most influential figures in Japanese Zen Buddhism, who regarded bodhicitta, working for the benefit of others, as the ultimate concern of Zen-training. While never having received formal dharma transmission, he is regarded as the reviver of the Japanese Rinzai school from a period of stagnation, focusing on rigorous training methods integrating meditation and koan practice.

bibliography.title: Rohatsu Exhortations By Hakuin Ekaku Zenji

teachers.name: Hakuun Yasutani
teachers.shortBio: Hakuun Yasutani  was a Sōtō Zen priest and the founder of the Sanbo Kyodan, a lay Japanese Zen group. Through his students Philip Kapleau and Taizan Maezumi, Yasutani has been one
biography: Hakuun Yasutani  was a Sōtō Zen priest and the founder of the Sanbo Kyodan, a lay Japanese Zen group. Through his students Philip Kapleau and Taizan Maezumi, Yasutani has been one of the principal forces in founding western (lay) Zen-practice.

teachers.name: Hamza Yusuf
teachers.shortBio: Hamza Yusuf is an American Islamic scholar. A pioneer of Islamic neo-traditionalism, he has promoted Islamic sciences and classical teaching methodologies throughout the world.
biography: Hamza Yusuf is an American Islamic scholar. A pioneer of Islamic neo-traditionalism, he has promoted Islamic sciences and classical teaching methodologies throughout the world.

teachers.name: Henri Nouwen
teachers.shortBio: Henri Jozef Machiel Nouwen was a Dutch Catholic priest, professor, writer and theologian. His interests were rooted primarily in psychology, pastoral ministry, spirituality, social
biography: Henri Jozef Machiel Nouwen was a Dutch Catholic priest, professor, writer and theologian. His interests were rooted primarily in psychology, pastoral ministry, spirituality, social justice and community. Over the course of his life, Nouwen was heavily influenced by the work of Anton Boisen, Thomas Merton, Rembrandt, Vincent van Gogh, and Jean Vanier.

teachers.name: Hildegard of Bingen
teachers.shortBio: Hildegard of Bingen, also known as the Sibyl of the Rhine, was a German Benedictine abbess and polymath active as a writer, composer, philosopher, mystic, visionary, and as a medic
biography: Hildegard of Bingen, also known as the Sibyl of the Rhine, was a German Benedictine abbess and polymath active as a writer, composer, philosopher, mystic, visionary, and as a medical writer and practitioner of the Catholic Church during the High Middle Ages. She is one of the best-known composers of sacred monophony, as well as the most recorded in modern history. A number of scholars have considered her to be the founder of scientific natural history in Germany.

teachers.name: Houn Jiyu-Kennett
teachers.shortBio: Hōun Jiyu-Kennett, born Peggy Teresa Nancy Kennett, was a British Buddhist abbess most famous for having been the first female rōshi to be sanctioned by the Sōtō School of Japan to
biography: Hōun Jiyu-Kennett, born Peggy Teresa Nancy Kennett, was a British Buddhist abbess most famous for having been the first female rōshi to be sanctioned by the Sōtō School of Japan to teach in Western countries.

teachers.name: Howard Thurman
teachers.shortBio: Howard Washington Thurman was an American author, philosopher, minister, theologian, Christian mystic, educator, and civil rights leader.
biography: Howard Washington Thurman was an American author, philosopher, minister, theologian, Christian mystic, educator, and civil rights leader.

teachers.name: Hsing Yun
teachers.shortBio: Hsing Yun was a Chinese Buddhist monk, teacher, and philanthropist based in Taiwan. He was the founder of the Fo Guang Shan Buddhist monastic order as well as the layperson-based B
biography: Hsing Yun was a Chinese Buddhist monk, teacher, and philanthropist based in Taiwan. He was the founder of the Fo Guang Shan Buddhist monastic order as well as the layperson-based Buddha's Light International Association. Hsing Yun was considered a major proponent of Humanistic Buddhism and one of the most influential teachers of modern Taiwanese Buddhism. In Taiwan, he was popularly referred to as one of the "Four Heavenly Kings" of Taiwanese Buddhism, along with his contemporaries: Master Sheng-yen of Dharma Drum Mountain, Master Cheng Yen of Tzu Chi and Master Wei Chueh of Chung Tai Shan.

biography: Hsuan Hua, also known as An Tzu, Tu Lun and Master Hua by his Western disciples, was a Chinese monk of Chan Buddhism and a contributing figure in bringing Chinese Buddhism to the United States in the late 20th century.

teachers.shortBio: Ibn 'Arabī was a Sunni Muslim Arab scholar, Sufi mystic, poet, and Muslim philosopher from al-Andalus, who exercised notable influence within Sufi metaphysics and Islamic thought i
biography: Ibn 'Arabī was a Sunni Muslim Arab scholar, Sufi mystic, poet, and Muslim philosopher from al-Andalus, who exercised notable influence within Sufi metaphysics and Islamic thought in general. There are 850 works attributed to Ibn 'Arabi, though only 700 of these are considered authentic, and only 400 are extant. His cosmological teachings became a dominant intellectual framework in many regions of the Muslim world.

biography: Idries Shah, also known as Idris Shah, Indries Shah, né Sayed Idries el-Hashimi and by the pen name Arkon Daraul, was an Afghan author, thinker and teacher in the Sufi tradition. Shah wrote over three dozen books on topics ranging from psychology and spirituality to travelogues and culture studies.

teachers.shortBio: Ignatius of Loyola, venerated as Saint Ignatius of Loyola, was a Spanish Basque Catholic priest and theologian, who, with six companions, founded the religious order of the Society
biography: Ignatius of Loyola, venerated as Saint Ignatius of Loyola, was a Spanish Basque Catholic priest and theologian, who, with six companions, founded the religious order of the Society of Jesus (Jesuits), and became its first Superior General, in Paris in 1541.

bibliography.title: The autobiography of St. Ignatius Loyola, with related documents

teachers.name: Ilia Delio
teachers.shortBio: Ilia Delio is a Franciscan sister in Washington, D.C., theologian, author, and university professor. She holds the Josephine C. Connelly Endowed Chair of Theology at Villanova Univ
biography: Ilia Delio is a Franciscan sister in Washington, D.C., theologian, author, and university professor. She holds the Josephine C. Connelly Endowed Chair of Theology at Villanova University. Delio is the founder of the Center for Christogenesis, an online educational resource, building upon the vision of Teilhard de Chardin in furtherance of the synthetic coherence of science and religion.

teachers.name: Ilona Ciunaite
teachers.shortBio: Ilona Ciunaite is a contemporary spiritual guide, known for her direct, inquiry-based approach that helps people see through the illusion of a separate self.
biography: Ilona Ciunaite is a Lithuanian-born spiritual guide and co-founder of Liberation Unleashed, a global community devoted to direct inquiry into the nature of self.
Her approach is practical and uncompromising: through guided dialogue she helps seekers look directly at experience until the illusion of a separate self is seen through.

teachers.name: Inayat Khan
teachers.shortBio: Inayat Khan Rehmat Khan, was an Indian professor of musicology, singer, exponent of the sarasvati vina, poet, philosopher, writer, and pioneer of the transmission of Sufism to the
biography: Inayat Khan Rehmat Khan, was an Indian professor of musicology, singer, exponent of the sarasvati vina, poet, philosopher, writer, and pioneer of the transmission of Sufism to the West. At the urging of his students, and on the basis of his ancestral Sufi tradition and four-fold training and authorisation at the hands of Sayyid Abu Hashim Madani of Hyderabad, he established an order of Sufism in London in 1914. By the time of his death in 1927, centers had been established throughout Europe and North America, and multiple volumes of his teachings had been published.

teachers.name: Jack Kornfield
teachers.shortBio: Jack Kornfield is an American psychologist, writer, and teacher in the Vipassana movement in American Theravada Buddhism. He trained as a Buddhist monk in Thailand, Burma and India
biography: Jack Kornfield is an American psychologist, writer, and teacher in the Vipassana movement in American Theravada Buddhism. He trained as a Buddhist monk in Thailand, Burma and India, first as a student of the Thai forest master Ajahn Chah and Mahasi Sayadaw of Burma. He has taught mindfulness meditation worldwide since 1974. In 1975, he co-founded the Insight Meditation Society in Barre, Massachusetts, with Sharon Salzberg and Joseph Goldstein, and subsequently in 1987, Spirit Rock Meditation Center in Woodacre, California. Kornfield has worked as a peacemaker and activist, organized teacher training, and led international gatherings of Buddhist teachers including the Dalai Lama.

teachers.name: Jagjot Singh
teachers.shortBio: Jagjot Singh is an Indian-born spiritual author and speaker who shares practical insights on non-duality (Advaita), emphasizing direct personal experience over traditional religious dogmas.
biography: Jagjot Singh is an Indian-born author and speaker who presents Advaita Vedanta in a contemporary, experiential way.
His work emphasizes direct personal experience over dogma, making non-dual understanding accessible through clear writing and dialogue.

bibliography.title: Meditation Is Not about Emptying Your Mind

teachers.name: James Low
teachers.shortBio: James Low is a teacher in the Byangter and Khordong lineages of Chhimed Rigdzin Lama, teaching Dzogchen in Europe and publishing translations and commentaries on the Great Perfection.
biography: James Low began studying Tibetan Buddhism in India in the 1960s, receiving teachings from Kalu Rinpoche, Chatral Rinpoche, Kanjur Rinpoche, and Dudjom Rinpoche. He became a close disciple of Chhimed Rigdzin Lama (C. R. Lama), living in his home in West Bengal for many years.

Authorized to teach by C. R. Lama in 1976, James has taught Dzogchen in Europe for over four decades. He publishes translations and commentaries through Simply Being and the Vajra Press, making Byangter and Khordong lineage texts accessible to Western practitioners.

teachers.name: Jean Klein
teachers.shortBio: Jean Klein was a French author, spiritual teacher and philosopher of Nondualism and Neo-Advaita. According to Jean Klein, it is only in a "spontaneous state of interior silence tha
biography: Jean Klein was a French author, spiritual teacher and philosopher of Nondualism and Neo-Advaita. According to Jean Klein, it is only in a "spontaneous state of interior silence that we can open ourselves to our true nature: the 'I Am' of pure consciousness."

teachers.name: Jean-Luc Achard
teachers.shortBio: Jean-Luc Achard is a French scholar-practitioner of Dzogchen, editor of the Revue d'Etudes Tibétaines and author of works on the Great Perfection and the Zhangzhung tradition.
biography: Jean-Luc Achard is a leading Western scholar of Dzogchen who has studied under Chögyal Namkhai Norbu and other Nyingma masters. He is the founder of Vajra Heart and editor of the Revue d'Etudes Tibétaines.

His publications include translations and studies of Dzogchen tantras, Zhangzhung Nyengyud texts, and works on trekchö and tögal. He teaches and conducts research on the philosophical and practice dimensions of the Great Perfection.

teachers.name: Jiddu Krishnamurti
teachers.shortBio: Jiddu Krishnamurti was an Indian spiritual figure, speaker, and writer. Adopted by members of the Theosophical Society as a child, Krishnamurti was raised to fill the mantle of the
biography: Jiddu Krishnamurti was an Indian spiritual figure, speaker, and writer. Adopted by members of the Theosophical Society as a child, Krishnamurti was raised to fill the mantle of the prophesied World Teacher, a role tasked with aiding humankind's spiritual evolution. In 1922, he began to suffer from painful, seizure-like mystical episodes that would produce a lasting change in his perception of reality. In 1929, he broke from the Theosophy movement and disbanded the Order of the Star in the East which had been formed around him. He spent the rest of his life speaking to groups and individuals around the world, hoping to contribute a radical transformation of mankind.

bibliography.title: What Are You Doing with Your Life?

teachers.name: Jigme Tromge Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Jigme Tromge Rinpoche is a Nyingma tulku and Dzogchen teacher, abbot of Jigme Gatseling Monastery in India and founder of Orgyen Dorje Den in California.
biography: Jigme Tromge Rinpoche was recognized as a tulku in the Nyingma tradition and trained at Jigme Gatseling Monastery. He received the complete transmissions of sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen from senior lineage masters including his root gurus in Tibet and India.
He established Orgyen Dorje Den, a Nyingma retreat center in Crescent City, California, where he teaches the Longchen Nyingthig and Dzogchen pith instructions to Western students.

teachers.name: Jim Newman
teachers.shortBio: Jim Newman is a non-duality teacher whose uncompromising, paradoxical message emphasizes that liberation lies in recognizing that "what is" needs no improvement.
biography: Jim Newman is an American non-duality teacher based in Vienna, Austria. His uncompromising message points directly to "what is" — not as a path to follow, but as the seamless, unconditional is-ness already present.

Influenced by the radical non-duality of Tony Parsons, Newman speaks spontaneously without prescription or expectation. He emphasizes that there is no separate self to become whole, and that the yearning for enlightenment perpetuates the very separation it seeks to resolve.

biography: Jim Tolles guides individuals toward freedom from egoic suffering through direct awareness and the loosening of personal identity.
His teaching is practical and compassionate, helping students see through the stories that sustain suffering.

biography: Mary Joanne Rogers Macy, known as Joanna Macy, was an American environmental activist, author and scholar of Buddhism, general systems theory and deep ecology. She was married to Francis Underhill Macy, the activist and Russian scholar who founded the Center for Safe Energy.

biography: John Cassian, also known as John the Ascetic and John Cassian the Roman,, was a Christian monk and theologian celebrated in both the Western and Eastern churches for his mystical writings. Cassian is noted for his role in bringing the ideas and practices of early Christian monasticism to the medieval West.

teachers.name: John Daido Loori
teachers.shortBio: John Daido Loori was a Zen Buddhist rōshi who served as the abbot of Zen Mountain Monastery and was the founder of the Mountains and Rivers Order and CEO of Dharma Communications.
biography: John Daido Loori was a Zen Buddhist rōshi who served as the abbot of Zen Mountain Monastery and was the founder of the Mountains and Rivers Order and CEO of Dharma Communications. Daido Loori received shiho from Taizan Maezumi in 1986 and also received a Dendo Kyoshi certificate formally from the Soto school of Japan in 1994. In 1997, he received dharma transmission in the Harada-Yasutani and Inzan lineages of Rinzai Zen as well. In 1996 he gave dharma transmission to his student Bonnie Myotai Treace, in 1997 to Geoffrey Shugen Arnold, and in 2009 to Konrad Ryushin Marchaj. In addition to his role as a Zen Buddhist priest, Loori was an exhibited photographer and author of more than twenty books and was an avid naturalist.

teachers.name: John Main
teachers.shortBio: John Douglas Main, OSB, was an English Roman Catholic priest and Benedictine monk who presented a way of Christian meditation which used a prayer-phrase or mantra. In 1975, Main be
biography: John Douglas Main, OSB, was an English Roman Catholic priest and Benedictine monk who presented a way of Christian meditation which used a prayer-phrase or mantra. In 1975, Main began Christian meditation groups which met at Ealing Abbey, his monastery in West London, England, and, later, in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. These were the origins of the ecumenical network of Christian meditation groups which have become the World Community for Christian Meditation (WCCM).

bibliography.title: Silence and stillness in every season

biography: John of the Cross was a Spanish Catholic priest, mystic, and Carmelite friar. He is a major figure of the Counter-Reformation in Spain, and he is one of the 38 Doctors of the Church.

teachers.name: Jon Kabat-Zinn
teachers.shortBio: Jon Kabat-Zinn is an American professor emeritus of medicine and the creator of the Stress Reduction Clinic and the Center for Mindfulness in Medicine, Health Care, and Society at
biography: Jon Kabat-Zinn is an American professor emeritus of medicine and the creator of the Stress Reduction Clinic and the Center for Mindfulness in Medicine, Health Care, and Society at the University of Massachusetts Medical School. Kabat-Zinn was a student of Zen Buddhist teachers such as Philip Kapleau, Thich Nhat Hanh, and Seung Sahn, and a founding member of Cambridge Zen Center. His practice of hatha yoga, Vipassanā and appreciation of the teachings of Soto Zen and Advaita Vedanta led him to integrate their teachings with scientific findings. He teaches mindfulness, which he says can help people cope with stress, anxiety, pain, and illness. The stress reduction program created by Kabat-Zinn, mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR), is offered by medical centers, hospitals, and health maintenance organizations, and is described in his book Full Catastrophe Living.

bibliography.title: Wherever You Go, There You Are

teachers.name: Joseph Goldstein
teachers.shortBio: Joseph Goldstein is one of the first American vipassana teachers, co-founder of the Insight Meditation Society (IMS) with Jack Kornfield and Sharon Salzberg, a contemporary author
biography: Joseph Goldstein is one of the first American vipassana teachers, co-founder of the Insight Meditation Society (IMS) with Jack Kornfield and Sharon Salzberg, a contemporary author of numerous popular books on Buddhism, a resident guiding teacher at IMS, and a leader of retreats worldwide on insight (vipassana) and lovingkindness (metta) meditation. An early promoter of insight practice and awareness, Goldstein is credited as an influence in the rise of mindfulness practices in the US since he started hosting workshops in 1974.

teachers.name: Joseph Rael
teachers.shortBio: Joseph Rael is a Native American ceremonial dancer, shaman, writer, and artist. He is also known as the founder of a global network of Sound Peace Chambers.
biography: Joseph Rael is a Native American ceremonial dancer, shaman, writer, and artist. He is also known as the founder of a global network of Sound Peace Chambers.

teachers.name: Josh Putnam
teachers.shortBio: Josh Putnam is a spiritual guide known for his authentic and intuitive approach to non-duality, helping individuals recognize their true nature through personalized guidance and presence.
biography: Josh Putnam offers an intuitive, perceptual approach to non-duality through personalized guidance and embodied presence.
His work helps individuals recognize their true nature beyond concepts, meeting each person where they are in their unfolding.

bibliography.title: Science Fiction of America's Gilded Age

teachers.name: Julian of Norwich
teachers.shortBio: Julian of Norwich, also known as Juliana of Norwich, the Lady Julian, Dame Julian or Mother Julian, was a medieval English Catholic anchoress. Her writings, now known as Revelation
biography: Julian of Norwich, also known as Juliana of Norwich, the Lady Julian, Dame Julian or Mother Julian, was a medieval English Catholic anchoress. Her writings, now known as Revelations of Divine Love, are the earliest surviving English-language works attributed to a woman. They are also the only surviving English-language works by an anchoress.

bibliography.title: The Showings of Julian of Norwich

teachers.name: Junayd of Baghdad
