biography: Junayd of Baghdad was a mystic and one of the most famous of the early Islamic saints. He is a central figure in the spiritual lineage of many Sufi orders.

teachers.name: Kabir Helminski
teachers.shortBio: Kabir Edmund Helminski is the author of a number of books on contemporary Sufism, a translator of Sufi poetry, and is the co-founder and co-director of Threshold Society. In 2001,
biography: Kabir Edmund Helminski is the author of a number of books on contemporary Sufism, a translator of Sufi poetry, and is the co-founder and co-director of Threshold Society. In 2001, Kabir was the first Muslim to deliver the Harold M. Wit Lectures on Spirituality in Contemporary Life at Harvard Divinity School. In 2007, he was also one of the original signatories of “A Common Word Between Us and You” an open letter by Islamic scholars to Christian leaders calling for peace and understanding.

teachers.name: Kallistos Ware
teachers.shortBio: Metropolitan Kallistos was an English bishop and theologian of the Eastern Orthodox Church. From 1982, he held the titular bishopric of Diokleia in Phrygia, later made a titular me
biography: Metropolitan Kallistos was an English bishop and theologian of the Eastern Orthodox Church. From 1982, he held the titular bishopric of Diokleia in Phrygia, later made a titular metropolitan bishopric in 2007, under the Ecumenical Patriarchate of Constantinople. He was one of the best-known modern Eastern Orthodox hierarchs and theologians. From 1966 to 2001, he was Spalding Lecturer of Eastern Orthodox Studies at the University of Oxford.

bibliography.title: Orthodox Theology in the TwentyFirst Century

teachers.name: Kalu Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Kalu Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist lama, meditation master, scholar and teacher. He was one of the first Tibetan masters to teach in the West.
biography: Kalu Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist lama, meditation master, scholar and teacher. He was one of the first Tibetan masters to teach in the West.

teachers.name: Kenneth Madden
teachers.shortBio: Kenneth Madden is a non-duality teacher who guides seekers to recognize their true nature beyond thought and ego, emphasizing direct experience and present-moment awareness.
biography: Kenneth Madden offers radical non-dual pointing from Ireland, emphasizing direct experience and present-moment awareness.
His teaching invites recognition of true nature beyond thought, belief, and the construct of a separate self.

bibliography.title: Essays in Imperial Government. Presented to Margery Perham.

teachers.name: Kevin Schanilec
teachers.shortBio: Kevin Schanilec is a spiritual teacher who has developed a clear, inquiry-based method rooted in the Buddhist model of the Ten Fetters.
biography: Kevin Schanilec teaches a path-based approach to awakening using the Ten Fetters framework as a practical map for liberation.
His method combines direct inquiry with structured guidance, helping students see through the fetters that maintain the illusion of separation.

teachers.name: Khemsar Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Khemsar Rinpoche is a Nyingma lama and Dzogchen teacher who studied under Chatral Rinpoche and other masters, teaching the Great Perfection in Nepal and internationally.
biography: Khemsar Rinpoche was born in Tibet and received teachings from Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche and other Nyingma masters. He completed extensive practice and retreat in the Dzogchen tradition.
Based in Nepal, he teaches trekchö and tögal, ngöndro, and the pith instructions of the Nyingma lineage to students in Asia and the West.

teachers.name: Khenchen Tsewang Gyatso
teachers.shortBio: Khenchen Tsewang Gyatso is a Nyingma khenpo and principal student of Penor Rinpoche who teaches Dzogchen, philosophy, and the Palyul tradition worldwide.
biography: Khenchen Tsewang Gyatso was born in Bhutan and trained at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche, completing the full khenpo curriculum in sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen. He is one of the most senior khenpos of the Palyul tradition.

He teaches extensively for the Palyul centers and Padmasambhava Buddhist Center (PBC), offering commentaries on Longchenpa, Mipham Rinpoche, and the Dzogchen instructions of the Nyingma lineage to students around the world.

bibliography.title: Emptiness : the Essence of the Heart Sutra

teachers.name: Khenpo Namdrol Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Khenpo Namdrol Rinpoche is a senior khenpo of Namdroling Monastery and one of the foremost living exponents of the Guhyagarbha Tantra and Dzogchen in the Palyul lineage.
biography: Khenpo Namdrol Rinpoche was born in Tibet and trained at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche, becoming one of the most respected khenpos of his generation. He is renowned for his mastery of the Guhyagarbha Tantra and Dzogchen teachings.
He teaches at Namdroling and Palyul centers worldwide, offering detailed scholastic and experiential guidance on the Nyingma tantras and the Great Perfection.

teachers.name: Khenpo Sonam
teachers.shortBio: Khenpo Sonam Tenphel is a senior Sakya khenpo and scholar who teaches Buddhist philosophy, logic, and meditation at Sakya College and affiliated centers.
biography: Khenpo Sonam Tenphel is a distinguished scholar of the Sakya tradition who completed the full monastic curriculum at Sakya College. He is known for his clear teaching of Madhyamaka, pramana, and the Lamdre system.
He teaches at Sakya College in Dehradun and at Sakya centers internationally, offering instruction in Buddhist philosophy and meditation to monastic and lay students.

bibliography.title: Amazing Treasury of the Sakya Lineage

teachers.name: Khenpo Sönam Tobgyal
teachers.shortBio: Khenpo Sönam Tobgyal is a senior Nyingma khenpo and close disciple of Dudjom Rinpoche who teaches Dzogchen and the Dudjom Tersar at Nyoshul Khenpo's monastery in Bhutan.
biography: Khenpo Sönam Tobgyal was born in Bhutan and trained under Dudjom Rinpoche, Nyoshul Khenpo, and other masters, receiving the complete Nyingma kama and terma transmissions.
He teaches at Nyoshul Khenpo's monastery in Paro, Bhutan, and at Nyingma centers worldwide, offering detailed instruction on Dzogchen, ngöndro, and the Dudjom Tersar treasure cycle.

teachers.name: Khenpo Tsewang Dongyal Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Khenpo Tsewang Dongyal Rinpoche is a Nyingma khenpo, abbot of Padmasambhava Buddhist Center, and co-author with Khenchen Palden Sherab of extensive teachings on Dzogchen and Nyingma philosophy.
biography: Khenpo Tsewang Dongyal Rinpoche was born in Kham, Tibet, and trained at Khawalung Monastery before fleeing to India. He and his brother Khenchen Palden Sherab Rinpoche were among the first Tibetan khenpos to teach extensively in the West.
As abbot of Padmasambhava Buddhist Center (PBC), he teaches Dzogchen, ngöndro, and the scholastic traditions of Longchenpa and Mipham Rinpoche at centers throughout the Americas and Europe.

bibliography.title: Six Bardos of the Tibetan Book of the Dead
Pointing Out the Nature of the Mind

teachers.name: Khenpo Tsultrim Gyamtso
teachers.shortBio: Khenpo Tsültrim Gyamtso Rinpoche was a Tibetan scholar yogi in the Kagyu tradition of Tibetan Buddhism. He taught widely in the West, often through songs of realization, his own as
biography: Khenpo Tsültrim Gyamtso Rinpoche was a Tibetan scholar yogi in the Kagyu tradition of Tibetan Buddhism. He taught widely in the West, often through songs of realization, his own as well as those composed by Milarepa and other masters of the past. "Tsültrim Gyamtso" translates to English as "Ocean of Ethical Conduct". He died on 22 June 2024, at the age of 90.

bibliography.title: Zhi hui zhi ri
bibliography.publisher: Fei hong guo ji zong jing xiao

teachers.name: Khentse Yangsi Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Khyentse Yangsi Rinpoche is the recognized incarnation of Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche and a principal lineage holder of the Khyentse tradition of Dzogchen and Rimé teachings.
biography: Khyentse Yangsi Rinpoche was recognized by Trulshik Rinpoche as the incarnation of Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche, one of the greatest Dzogchen masters of the twentieth century. He was enthroned at Shechen Monastery and trained under the guidance of senior Khyentse lineage masters.
He teaches the transmissions of the Khyentse treasure cycles, Longchen Nyingthig, and Dzogchen pith instructions through Siddhartha's Intent and affiliated centers worldwide.

teachers.name: Kilung Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Dza Kilung Rinpoche Jigme Tenzin Chödrak is the fifth incarnation of Getse Lama Jigme Ngotsar and head of Kilung Monastery, a Longchen Nyingthig and Dzogchen lineage holder.
biography: H.E. Dza Kilung Rinpoche was recognized as the fifth incarnation of Getse Lama Jigme Ngotsar Gyatso, one of the four heart sons of Rigdzin Jigme Lingpa. He has rebuilt Kilung Monastery in Dzachuka, Tibet, as a center of Longchen Nyingthig study and practice.

He founded the Kilung Foundation and Pema Kilaya sangha, teaching Dzogchen and meditation at Yeshe Long Buddhist Center on Whidbey Island and at centers worldwide. He is the author of The Relaxed Mind (Shambhala, 2015).

teachers.name: Kogen Czarnik
teachers.shortBio: Kogen Czarnik is a Soto Zen priest in the lineage of Tangen Harada Roshi, who works as a translator and editor of Zen texts.
biography: Kogen Czarnik is a Soto Zen priest in the lineage of Tangen Harada Roshi.
He is known for his scholarly work as a translator and editor of Zen texts, bridging classical Japanese Zen literature with contemporary practice.

bibliography.title: In This Body, in This Lifetime
Throw Yourself into the House of Buddha

teachers.name: Kyabgön Phakchok Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Phakchok Rinpoche is a teacher of the Nyingma lineage and chief lineage holder of the Taklung Kagyu lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. He is Vajra Master of Ka-Nying Shedrup Ling monaste
biography: Phakchok Rinpoche is a teacher of the Nyingma lineage and chief lineage holder of the Taklung Kagyu lineage of Tibetan Buddhism. He is Vajra Master of Ka-Nying Shedrup Ling monastery, abbot of several monasteries in Nepal, and assists monasteries and practice centers in Tibet. In addition, he serves as Director of the Chokgyur Lingpa Foundation, a nonprofit organization engaged in a wide range of humanitarian projects.

teachers.name: Lama Dechen Yeshe Wangmo
teachers.shortBio: Lama Dechen Yeshe Wangmo is a Western-born Nyingma lama and disciple of Thinley Norbu Rinpoche who teaches Dzogchen and the Dudjom Tersar lineage.
biography: Lama Dechen Yeshe Wangmo studied under Thinley Norbu Rinpoche and other Nyingma masters, receiving the complete Dudjom Tersar transmissions and Dzogchen instructions.
She teaches through the Utpala community, offering ngöndro, sadhana, and pith instructions of the Dudjom lineage to students in North America and Europe.

teachers.name: Lama Jigme
teachers.shortBio: Lama Jigme Rinpoche is an author and teacher in the Karma Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. Born into the family of Rangjung Rigpe Dorje, 16th Karmapa, he is the brother of the lat
biography: Lama Jigme Rinpoche is an author and teacher in the Karma Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. Born into the family of Rangjung Rigpe Dorje, 16th Karmapa, he is the brother of the late 14th Shamar Rinpoche, Mipham Chokyi Lodro. The 16th Karmapa appointed Lama Jigme Rinpoche as his European representative. He asked him to oversee the development of a shedra (university), library, retreat center, and monastery at Dhagpo Kagyu Ling in Dordogne, France, where Jigme Rinpoche is currently the main representative of Trinley Thaye Dorje and head of the monastery.

bibliography.title: Dzogchen Preliminary Practice of the Longchen Nyingtik

teachers.name: Lama Jigme Tenzin
teachers.shortBio: Lama Jigme Tenzin is a Nyingma lama and Dzogchen teacher who guides students in meditation and the preliminary practices of the Great Perfection.
biography: Lama Jigme Tenzin was trained in the Nyingma tradition in Tibet and Nepal, receiving empowerments and pith instructions from senior lineage masters.
He teaches ngöndro, shamatha, and Dzogchen introduction to students in Nepal and internationally.

teachers.name: Lama Lena
teachers.shortBio: Lama Lena (Yeshe Kaytup) is a lineage holder of Dzogchen and Mahamudra, trained under Wangdor Rinpoche after studying with Lama Thubten Yeshe in Nepal.
biography: Lama Lena began studying with Lama Thubten Yeshe at Kopan Monastery in 1972. She then spent seven years in retreat in a cave above Tso Pema, India, under her root teacher Wangdor Rinpoche, serving as his translator and assistant for twenty-five years.

At the request of Zigar Choktrul Rinpoche and Wangdor Rinpoche, she teaches direct mind perception (Dzogchen and Mahamudra) internationally. She also studied Chinese medicine and provides medical assistance in Tso Pema and New Orleans.

teachers.name: Lama Namdrol Zangpo
teachers.shortBio: Lama Namdrol Zangpo is a Nyingma lama and Dzogchen practitioner in the Bhutanese tradition, trained under senior masters of the Drukpa and Nyingma lineages.
biography: Lama Namdrol Zangpo trained in the Nyingma and Dzogchen traditions in Bhutan, receiving transmissions from senior lineage holders and completing extensive retreat practice.
He teaches Dzogchen pith instructions and supports Dharma communities in Bhutan, emphasizing the integration of meditation and daily life.

teachers.name: Lama Surya Das
teachers.shortBio: Surya Das is an American lama in the Tibetan Buddhist tradition. He is a poet, chantmaster, spiritual activist, author of many popular works on Buddhism, meditation teacher, and sp
biography: Surya Das is an American lama in the Tibetan Buddhist tradition. He is a poet, chantmaster, spiritual activist, author of many popular works on Buddhism, meditation teacher, and spokesperson for Buddhism in the West. He has been accused of sexual misconduct by several of his former students.

teachers.name: Lama Tenzin Samphel
teachers.shortBio: Lama Tenzin Samphel is a Nyingma lama and translator who studied under Kyabjé Dudjom Rinpoche and teaches Dzogchen and the Dudjom Tersar lineage in Canada.
biography: Lama Tenzin Samphel was born in Tibet and trained in the Nyingma tradition, receiving empowerments and instructions from Kyabjé Dudjom Rinpoche and other masters before settling in Canada.
He teaches through Rigpa Web and affiliated centers in Montreal, offering ngöndro, sadhana practice, and Dzogchen pith instructions from the Dudjom Tersar lineage.

teachers.name: Lama Tony Duff
teachers.shortBio: Lama Tony Duff is an Australian translator and teacher who founded the Padma Karpo Translation Committee, specializing in Mahamudra and Dzogchen texts including the Longchen Nyingthig.
biography: Lama Tony Duff was ordained as the first Tibetan Buddhist monk in Australia and studied at Chenrezig Institute under Lama Yeshe and Lama Zopa. He later became a senior student of Chögyam Trungpa Rinpoche and served as Tsoknyi Rinpoche's personal translator for nearly ten years.

He founded the Padma Karpo Translation Committee in 1993, producing major translations of Dzogchen and Mahamudra texts, Tibetan-English dictionaries, and language software. He teaches the Longchen Nyingthig and Dzogchen pith instructions internationally.

teachers.name: Lama Zopa Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Thubten Zopa Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist lama in the Gelug school. He is known for founding the Foundation for the Preservation of the Mahayana Tradition and Maitripa College i
biography: Thubten Zopa Rinpoche was a Tibetan Buddhist lama in the Gelug school. He is known for founding the Foundation for the Preservation of the Mahayana Tradition and Maitripa College in Portland, Oregon.

teachers.name: Latri Nyima Dakpa Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Latri Nyima Dakpa Rinpoche is a senior Bönpo lama and head of the Latri lineage who teaches Dzogchen and the Zhangzhung sNyan-rgyud tradition internationally.
biography: Latri Nyima Dakpa Rinpoche was born into the Latri family lineage of Bön in Kham, Tibet. He trained at Menri Monastery under H.H. the 33rd Menri Trizin and other masters, completing the full curriculum of sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen.
He founded the Bon Institute and Latri Kalpa centers, teaching the Zhangzhung Nyengyud Dzogchen cycle and Bön spiritual traditions to students in Asia, Europe, and the Americas.

teachers.name: Laurence Freeman
teachers.shortBio: Laurence Freeman OSB is an English Benedictine monk and Catholic priest. He belongs to the Monastery of Sta Maria di Pilastrello, in Italy, of the Congregation of Monte Oliveto Mag
biography: Laurence Freeman OSB is an English Benedictine monk and Catholic priest. He belongs to the Monastery of Sta Maria di Pilastrello, in Italy, of the Congregation of Monte Oliveto Maggiore. He is the director of the World Community for Christian Meditation (WCCM) prior of its Benedictine oblate community and director of Bonnevaux, the international centre of the WCCM in France.

teachers.name: Leonard Crow Dog
teachers.shortBio: Leonard Crow Dog was a Lakota spiritual leader and medicine man. He became well known during the Lakota takeover and occupation of the town of Wounded Knee on the Pine Ridge Indian
biography: Leonard Crow Dog was a Lakota spiritual leader and medicine man. He became well known during the Lakota takeover and occupation of the town of Wounded Knee on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation in South Dakota in 1973, known as the Wounded Knee Incident. Through his writings and teachings, he sought to unify Indian people of all nations. As a practitioner of traditional herbal medicine and a leader of Sun Dance ceremonies, Crow Dog was dedicated to keeping Lakota traditions alive.

teachers.name: Lho Ontül Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Lho Ontül Rinpoche is a Nyingma tulku and Dzogchen master of the Golok region, holder of the Ontül lineage of treasure teachings.
biography: Lho Ontül Rinpoche was recognized as the incarnation of the Ontül lineage in Golok, eastern Tibet. He received the complete Nyingma and treasure transmissions from his root teachers in the region.
He teaches Dzogchen pith instructions and supports monastic communities, preserving the Ontül lineage of terma revelations and practice traditions.

teachers.name: Lisa Cairns
teachers.shortBio: Lisa Cairns is a contemporary non-duality teacher who shares an open, spontaneous message pointing to the illusory nature of the separate self and the ever-present freedom of life as it is.
biography: Lisa Cairns offers an open, spontaneous style of non-dual pointing that emphasizes the illusory nature of the separate self.
Her gatherings invite a direct recognition of the freedom and wholeness already present in life as it is, without methods or progressive stages.

teachers.name: Llewellyn Vaughan-Lee
teachers.shortBio: Llewellyn Vaughan-Lee is a Sufi mystic and lineage successor in the Naqshbandiyya-Mujaddidiyya Sufi Order. He is an extensive lecturer and author of several books about Sufism, mys
biography: Llewellyn Vaughan-Lee is a Sufi mystic and lineage successor in the Naqshbandiyya-Mujaddidiyya Sufi Order. He is an extensive lecturer and author of several books about Sufism, mysticism, dreamwork and spirituality.

teachers.name: Longchenpa
teachers.shortBio: Longchen Rabjam Drimé Özer, or simply Longchenpa was a Tibetan scholar-yogi of the Nyingma school, the 'Old School' of Tibetan Buddhism. According to tibetologist David Germano, Lo
biography: Longchen Rabjam Drimé Özer, or simply Longchenpa was a Tibetan scholar-yogi of the Nyingma school, the 'Old School' of Tibetan Buddhism. According to tibetologist David Germano, Longchenpa's work led to the dominance of the Longchen Nyingthig lineage of Dzogchen, the Great Perfection, over the other Dzogchen traditions. He is also responsible for the scholastic systematization of Dzogchen thought within the context of the wider Tibetan Vajrayana tradition of Buddhist philosophy.

bibliography.title: Finding Rest in the Nature of the Mind

teachers.name: Lopon Ogyan Tenzin
teachers.shortBio: Lopon Ogyan Tenzin Jigme is a Nyingma lopön trained at Namdroling Monastery who teaches Dzogchen and ritual at Thubten Chöling and Tsechen Kunchab Ling.
biography: Lopon Ogyan Tenzin Jigme Rinpoche completed advanced training at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche, specializing in ritual, philosophy, and Dzogchen practice.
He teaches at Thubten Chöling in upstate New York and Tsechen Kunchab Ling in Santa Fe, offering guidance on ngöndro, sadhana, and the Nyingma path of the Great Perfection.

teachers.name: Lopön Tenzin Namdak
teachers.shortBio: Lopön Tenzin Namdak was a Tibetan religious leader and the most senior authority and teacher of Bon, in particular of Dzogchen and the Mother Tantras.
biography: Lopön Tenzin Namdak was a Tibetan religious leader and the most senior authority and teacher of Bon, in particular of Dzogchen and the Mother Tantras.

bibliography.title: Vier Worte, das Rad der Bonpo-Lehre
bibliography.publisher: Foundation for the Preservation of Yungdrung Bon

teachers.name: M. Basil Pennington
teachers.shortBio: M. Basil Pennington, O.C.S.O. (1931–2005) was an American Trappist priest and leading spiritual writer, speaker, teacher, and director. He became known internationally as one of th
biography: M. Basil Pennington, O.C.S.O. (1931–2005) was an American Trappist priest and leading spiritual writer, speaker, teacher, and director. He became known internationally as one of the major proponents of the centering prayer movement begun at St. Joseph's Abbey in Spencer, Massachusetts, during the 1970s.

teachers.name: Mad Bear Anderson
teachers.shortBio: Mad Bear Anderson is a teacher in the Indigenous Wisdom tradition.
biography: Mad Bear Anderson is a teacher in the Indigenous Wisdom tradition.

teachers.name: Maha Ghosananda
teachers.shortBio: Maha Ghosananda was a highly revered Cambodian Buddhist monk in the Theravada tradition, who served as the Patriarch (Sangharaja) of Cambodian Buddhism during the Khmer Rouge perio
biography: Maha Ghosananda was a highly revered Cambodian Buddhist monk in the Theravada tradition, who served as the Patriarch (Sangharaja) of Cambodian Buddhism during the Khmer Rouge period and post-communist transition period of Cambodian history. His Pali monastic name, 'Mahā Ghosānanda', means "great joyful proclaimer". He was well known in Cambodia for his annual peace marches.

teachers.name: Mahasi Sayadaw
teachers.shortBio: Mahāsī Sayādaw U Sobhana was a Burmese Theravada Buddhist monk and meditation master who had a significant impact on the teaching of Vipassana (insight) meditation in the West and
biography: Mahāsī Sayādaw U Sobhana was a Burmese Theravada Buddhist monk and meditation master who had a significant impact on the teaching of Vipassana (insight) meditation in the West and throughout Asia.

bibliography.title: Great Discourse on the Turning of the Wheel of Dhamma

teachers.name: Malidoma Patrice Somé
teachers.shortBio: Malidoma Patrice Somé (1956–2021) was a writer and workshop leader, primarily in the field of spirituality. Born in a Dagara community in Dano, Burkina Faso, West Africa, he was ra
biography: Malidoma Patrice Somé (1956–2021) was a writer and workshop leader, primarily in the field of spirituality. Born in a Dagara community in Dano, Burkina Faso, West Africa, he was raised by Jesuit priests from the age of four, pursued higher education in the West, and spent most of his adult life in the United States and Europe.

bibliography.title: Die Kraft des Rituals. Afrikanische Traditionen für die westliche Welt.
Of water and the spirit
bibliography.publisher: Diederichs GmbH & Co. KG, Verlag Eugen

teachers.name: Mansur Al-Hallaj
teachers.shortBio: Mansour al-Hallaj or Mansour Hallaj was a mystic, poet, and teacher of Sufism. He was best known for his saying "I am the Truth" ("Ana'l-Ḥaqq"), which many saw as a claim to divini
biography: Mansour al-Hallaj or Mansour Hallaj was a mystic, poet, and teacher of Sufism. He was best known for his saying "I am the Truth" ("Ana'l-Ḥaqq"), which many saw as a claim to divinity, while others interpreted it as an instance of annihilation of the ego, which allowed God to speak through him. Al-Hallaj gained a wide following as a preacher before he became implicated in power struggles of the Abbasid court and was executed after a long period of confinement on religious and political charges. Although most of his Sufi contemporaries disapproved of his actions, Hallaj later became a major figure in the Sufi tradition.

bibliography.title: Ḥaqāʼiq al-tafsīr, aw, Khalq khalāʼiq al-Qurʼān wa-al-iʻtibār
Dīwān al-Ḥallāj Abī al-Mughīth al-Ḥusayn ibn Manṣūr ibn Maḥmá al-Bayḍāwī

teachers.name: María Sabina
teachers.shortBio: María Sabina Magdalena García was a Mazatec sabia and poet who lived in Huautla de Jiménez, a town in the Sierra Mazateca area of the Mexican state of Oaxaca in southern Mexico. He
biography: María Sabina Magdalena García was a Mazatec sabia and poet who lived in Huautla de Jiménez, a town in the Sierra Mazateca area of the Mexican state of Oaxaca in southern Mexico. Her healing sacred mushroom ceremonies, called veladas, were based on the use of psilocybin mushrooms, particularly Psilocybe caerulescens, a sacred mushroom important to the Mazatecs. María Sabina's veladas contributed to the popularization of indigenous Mexican ritual use of entheogenic mushrooms among westerners, though this was not her intent.

bibliography.title: Performance and Performativity in Contemporary Indian Fiction in English

teachers.name: Marpa Lotsawa
teachers.shortBio: Marpa Lotsāwa, sometimes known fully as Marpa Chökyi Lodrö or commonly as Marpa the Translator, was a Tibetan Buddhist teacher credited with the transmission of many Vajrayana teac
biography: Marpa Lotsāwa, sometimes known fully as Marpa Chökyi Lodrö or commonly as Marpa the Translator, was a Tibetan Buddhist teacher credited with the transmission of many Vajrayana teachings from India, including the teachings and lineages of Mahamudra. Due to this, the Kagyu lineage, which he founded, is often called Marpa Kagyu in his honour.

teachers.name: Martin Lings
teachers.shortBio: Martin Lings, also known as Abū Bakr Sirāj ad-Dīn, was an English writer, Islamic scholar, and philosopher. A student of the Swiss metaphysician Frithjof Schuon and an authority on
biography: Martin Lings, also known as Abū Bakr Sirāj ad-Dīn, was an English writer, Islamic scholar, and philosopher. A student of the Swiss metaphysician Frithjof Schuon and an authority on the work of William Shakespeare, he is best known as the author of Muhammad: His Life Based on the Earliest Sources.

teachers.name: Martín Prechtel
teachers.shortBio: Martín Prechtel is an American author and educator.
biography: Martín Prechtel is an American author and educator.

teachers.name: Mata Amritanandamayi
teachers.shortBio: Sri Mātā Amritānandamayī Devi, often known as Amma ("Mother"), is an Indian Hindu spiritual leader, godwoman, guru and humanitarian. She is the chancellor of Amrita Vishwa Vidyapee
biography: Sri Mātā Amritānandamayī Devi, often known as Amma ("Mother"), is an Indian Hindu spiritual leader, godwoman, guru and humanitarian. She is the chancellor of Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, a multi-campus research university.

teachers.name: Matt Garrett
teachers.shortBio: Matt Garrett is a non-duality teacher whose approach leads seekers to dissolve the illusion of separation and integrate the freedom and mystery of what is into everyday life.
biography: Matt Garrett teaches an awareness-based approach to non-duality that integrates recognition of freedom into everyday life.
His work emphasizes dissolving the illusion of separation while honoring the mystery and immediacy of experience as it is.

bibliography.title: Call of the Crown : Part I

teachers.name: Matthew Fox
teachers.shortBio: Timothy James "Matthew" Fox is an American priest and theologian. Formerly a member of the Dominican Order within the Catholic Church, he became a priest in the Episcopal Church fo
biography: Timothy James "Matthew" Fox is an American priest and theologian. Formerly a member of the Dominican Order within the Catholic Church, he became a priest in the Episcopal Church following his expulsion from the order in 1993.

bibliography.title: Sins of the spirit, blessings of the flesh

teachers.name: Meher Baba
teachers.shortBio: Meher Baba was an Indian spiritual master who said he was the Avatar, or the total manifestation of God in human form. A spiritual figure of the 20th century, he had a following of
biography: Meher Baba was an Indian spiritual master who said he was the Avatar, or the total manifestation of God in human form. A spiritual figure of the 20th century, he had a following of hundreds of thousands of people, mostly in India, with a smaller number of followers in North America, Europe, South America, and Australia.

teachers.name: Meister Eckhart
teachers.shortBio: Eckhart von Hochheim, commonly known as Meister Eckhart, Master Eckhart or Eckehart, claimed original name Johannes Eckhart, was a German Catholic priest, theologian, philosopher a
biography: Eckhart von Hochheim, commonly known as Meister Eckhart, Master Eckhart or Eckehart, claimed original name Johannes Eckhart, was a German Catholic priest, theologian, philosopher and mystic. He was born near Gotha in the Landgraviate of Thuringia in the Holy Roman Empire.

bibliography.title: Meister Eckhart, from whom God hid nothing

teachers.name: Menri Lopon Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Menri Lopon Rinpoche is the principal lopön (head teacher) of Menri Monastery, the central institution of the Yungdrung Bön tradition and its Dzogchen teachings.
biography: Menri Lopon Rinpoche holds the senior teaching position at Menri Monastery in Dolanji, India, the seat of the Bön tradition's spiritual head, the Menri Trizin. He is responsible for the advanced training of monks in Bön philosophy, tantra, and Dzogchen.

He teaches the Zhangzhung Nyengyud and other Dzogchen cycles of the Bön tradition, preserving and transmitting the ancient teachings of the Great Perfection to monastic and lay practitioners worldwide.

teachers.name: Milarepa
teachers.shortBio: Jetsun Milarepa was a Tibetan siddha, who was famously known as a murderer when he was a young man, before turning to Buddhism and becoming a highly accomplished Buddhist disciple.
biography: Jetsun Milarepa was a Tibetan siddha, who was famously known as a murderer when he was a young man, before turning to Buddhism and becoming a highly accomplished Buddhist disciple. He is generally considered one of Tibet's most famous yogis and spiritual poets, whose teachings are known among several schools of Tibetan Buddhism. He was a student of Marpa Lotsawa, and a major figure in the history of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. He is also famous for the feat of climbing Mount Kailash.

teachers.name: Miranda Macpherson
teachers.shortBio: Miranda Macpherson is a spiritual teacher and interfaith minister known for her feminine approach to nondual realization, emphasizing the practice of Ego Relaxation.
biography: Miranda Macpherson is a spiritual teacher and interfaith minister known for her feminine, embodied approach to nondual realization.
Her signature practice of Ego Relaxation guides students into direct experience through softening rather than striving.

teachers.name: Mirra Alfassa
teachers.shortBio: Mirra Alfassa, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère, was a French-Indian spiritual guru, occultist and yoga teacher, and a collaborator of Sri Aurobindo, who considered
biography: Mirra Alfassa, known to her followers as The Mother or La Mère, was a French-Indian spiritual guru, occultist and yoga teacher, and a collaborator of Sri Aurobindo, who considered her to be of equal yogic stature to him and called her by the name "The Mother" or "Shri Maa".

bibliography.title: Commentaries on the Dhammapada, US Edition (Buddhism)

teachers.name: Moinuddin Chishti
teachers.shortBio: Mu'in al-Din Hasan Chishti Sijzi, known reverentially as Khawaja Gharib Nawaz, was a Persian Sayyid Islamic scholar and mystic from Sistan, who eventually ended up settling in the
biography: Mu'in al-Din Hasan Chishti Sijzi, known reverentially as Khawaja Gharib Nawaz, was a Persian Sayyid Islamic scholar and mystic from Sistan, who eventually ended up settling in the Indian subcontinent in the early 13th-century, where he promulgated the Chishtiyya order of Islamic mysticism. This particular Tariqa (order) became the dominant Islamic spiritual order in medieval India. Most of the Indian Sunni saints are Chishti in their affiliation, including Nizamuddin Awliya and Amir Khusrow.

bibliography.title: Le Livre de la guérison soufie

teachers.name: Mooji
teachers.shortBio: Mooji is a Jamaican spiritual teacher of Advaita based in the UK and Portugal. He gives talks (satsang) and conducts retreats. Mooji lives in Portugal, at Monte Sahaja.
biography: Mooji is a Jamaican spiritual teacher of Advaita based in the UK and Portugal. He gives talks (satsang) and conducts retreats. Mooji lives in Portugal, at Monte Sahaja.

bibliography.title: Vaster Than Sky, Greater Than Space

teachers.name: Muktananda
teachers.shortBio: Muktananda, born Krishna Rai, was a yoga guru and the founder of Siddha Yoga. He was a disciple of Bhagavan Nityananda. He wrote books on the subjects of Kundalini Shakti, Vedanta,
biography: Muktananda, born Krishna Rai, was a yoga guru and the founder of Siddha Yoga. He was a disciple of Bhagavan Nityananda. He wrote books on the subjects of Kundalini Shakti, Vedanta, and Kashmir Shaivism, including a spiritual autobiography entitled The Play of Consciousness. In honorific style, he is often referred to as Swami Muktananda, or Baba Muktananda, or in a familiar way just Baba.

teachers.name: Nagarjuna
teachers.shortBio: Nāgārjuna was a philosopher and Mahāyāna Buddhist monk from South India, considered the founder of the Madhyamaka school.
biography: Nāgārjuna was a philosopher and Mahāyāna Buddhist monk from South India, considered the founder of the Madhyamaka school.

teachers.name: Namkha Drimed Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Namkha Drimed Rinpoche is the head of the Ripa lineage and a holder of the Nyen Gyud Samba Melong terma cycle, teaching Dzogchen and Mahamudra internationally.
biography: Namkha Drimed Rinpoche was recognized as the incarnation of a great tertön and trained under senior Nyingma and Kagyu masters. He is the supreme head of the Ripa monastic lineage with seats in Tibet and Nepal.
He teaches the Ripa treasure cycles, including Dzogchen and Mahamudra instructions, through Ripa International centers in North America, Europe, and Asia.

teachers.name: Naropa
teachers.shortBio: Nāropā was an Indian Buddhist Mahasiddha. He was the disciple of Tilopa and brother, or some sources say partner and pupil, of Niguma. As an Indian Mahasiddha, Naropa's instruction
biography: Nāropā was an Indian Buddhist Mahasiddha. He was the disciple of Tilopa and brother, or some sources say partner and pupil, of Niguma. As an Indian Mahasiddha, Naropa's instructions inform Vajrayana, particularly his six yogas of Naropa relevant to the completion stage of anuttarayogatantra.
He was also one of the "gatekeepers" of Vikramashila monastery and was also associated with Nalanda monastery where he rose to the position of abbot.

bibliography.title: Naropa lectures, 1981
bibliography.publisher: Center for the Humanities, the Graduate Center, The City University of New York

teachers.name: Neem Karoli Baba
teachers.shortBio: Neem Karoli Baba or Neeb Karori Baba, also known to his followers as Maharaj-ji, was a Hindu guru and a devotee of the Hindu deity Hanuman.
biography: Neem Karoli Baba or Neeb Karori Baba, also known to his followers as Maharaj-ji, was a Hindu guru and a devotee of the Hindu deity Hanuman.

teachers.name: Ngakchang Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Ngakchang Rinpoche (Ngakpa Chögyam) is a Nyingma ngakpa lama and co-founder of the Aro gTér lineage, teaching Dzogchen and the non-monastic tantric tradition in the West.
biography: Ngakchang Rinpoche, also known as Ngakpa Chögyam, is a British-born Nyingma ngakpa lama who received transmission of the Aro gTér cycle, a terma tradition emphasizing Dzogchen and the path of the white-robed yogic practitioner.
With Khandro Déchen, he co-founded the Aro gTér tradition, teaching Dzogchen, thögal, and the ngakpa path through centers in the UK and internationally.

teachers.name: Nida Chenagtsang
teachers.shortBio: Nida Chenagtsang is a Tibetan ngakpa physician, scholar, and founder of Sorig Khang International, teaching Dzogchen, Yuthok Nyingthig, and Tibetan medicine.
biography: Dr. Nida Chenagtsang was born in Amdo and trained as a ngakpa (non-monastic tantric practitioner) and physician in the Yuthok Nyingthig medical-spiritual lineage. He received Dzogchen and medicine teachings from multiple masters in Tibet and India.
He founded Sorig Khang International and the Ngakmang Institute, teaching Tibetan medicine, Vajrayana, and the Yuthok Nyingthig Dzogchen cycle to students worldwide.

bibliography.title: Yuthok Nyingthig : Ati Yoga and Mahamudra Commentary

teachers.name: Nisargadatta Maharaj
teachers.shortBio: Nisargadatta Maharaj was an Indian guru of nonduality, belonging to the Inchagiri Sampradaya, a lineage of teachers from the Navnath Sampradaya.
biography: Nisargadatta Maharaj was an Indian guru of nonduality, belonging to the Inchagiri Sampradaya, a lineage of teachers from the Navnath Sampradaya.

teachers.name: Nizamuddin Auliya
teachers.shortBio: Khawaja Syed Muhammad b. Ahmad Ali al-Badaoni al-Bukhari, popularly called Nizamuddin Auliya, also known as Hazrat Nizamuddin, Sultan-ul-Mashaikh and Mahbub-e-Ilahi, was an Indian
biography: Khawaja Syed Muhammad b. Ahmad Ali al-Badaoni al-Bukhari, popularly called Nizamuddin Auliya, also known as Hazrat Nizamuddin, Sultan-ul-Mashaikh and Mahbub-e-Ilahi, was an Indian Sunni Muslim scholar, Sufi saint of the Chishti Order, and is one of the most famous Sufis from the Indian subcontinent. His predecessors were Fariduddin Ganjshakar, Qutbuddin Bakhtiyar Kaki, and Moinuddin Chishti, who were the masters of the Chishti spiritual chain or silsila in the Indian subcontinent.

teachers.name: Noah Levine
teachers.shortBio: Noah Levine is an American Buddhist teacher and author, son of Stephen Levine. As a counselor known for his philosophical alignment with Buddhism and punk ideology, he identifies h
biography: Noah Levine is an American Buddhist teacher and author, son of Stephen Levine. As a counselor known for his philosophical alignment with Buddhism and punk ideology, he identifies his Buddhist beliefs and practices with both the Theravada and Mahayana traditions. He has written several books on Buddhism and Buddhist practice including Refuge Recovery: A Buddhist Path to Recovering from Addiction.

teachers.shortBio: Nuh Ha Mim Keller is an American Islamic scholar, teacher and author who lives in Amman. He is a translator of a number of Islamic books.
biography: Nuh Ha Mim Keller is an American Islamic scholar, teacher and author who lives in Amman. He is a translator of a number of Islamic books.

teachers.name: Nyanaponika Thera
teachers.shortBio: Nyanaponika Thera or Nyanaponika Mahathera was a German–Sri Lankan Theravada Buddhist monk and scholar who, after ordaining in Sri Lanka, later became the co-founder of the Buddhis
biography: Nyanaponika Thera or Nyanaponika Mahathera was a German–Sri Lankan Theravada Buddhist monk and scholar who, after ordaining in Sri Lanka, later became the co-founder of the Buddhist Publication Society and author of numerous seminal books and articles on Theravada Buddhism. He mentored and taught a whole generation of Western Buddhist leaders such as Bhikkhu Bodhi.

teachers.name: Ogyen Trinley Dorje
teachers.shortBio: Ogyen Trinley Dorje, also written as Urgyen Trinley Dorje is a claimant to the title of 17th Karmapa.
biography: Ogyen Trinley Dorje, also written as Urgyen Trinley Dorje is a claimant to the title of 17th Karmapa.

teachers.name: Oren Lyons
teachers.shortBio: Oren R. Lyons Jr. is a Haudenosaunee Faithkeeper of the Turtle Clan of both the Onondaga Nation and the Seneca Nation of the Six Nations of the Grand River. For more than 14 years
biography: Oren R. Lyons Jr. is a Haudenosaunee Faithkeeper of the Turtle Clan of both the Onondaga Nation and the Seneca Nation of the Six Nations of the Grand River. For more than 14 years he has been a member of the Indigenous Peoples of the Human Rights Commission of the United Nations and has had other leadership roles.

bibliography.title: Exiled in the Land of the Free

teachers.name: Orgyen Chowang
teachers.shortBio: Orgyen Chowang Rinpoche is a Nyingma lama and disciple of Penor Rinpoche who teaches Dzogchen through the Pristine Mind Foundation.
biography: Orgyen Chowang Rinpoche was recognized as a tulku and trained at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche, completing extensive study and retreat in the Nyingma tradition.
He founded the Pristine Mind Foundation and teaches Dzogchen meditation and the nature of mind to students worldwide, emphasizing practical integration of awareness in everyday life.

bibliography.title: Releasing the Knot of the Mind

teachers.name: Padmasambhava
teachers.shortBio: Padmasambhava, also known as Guru Rinpoche, was a semi-legendary tantric Buddhist Vajra master from medieval India, who according to hagiographical sources fully revealed the Vajra
biography: Padmasambhava, also known as Guru Rinpoche, was a semi-legendary tantric Buddhist Vajra master from medieval India, who according to hagiographical sources fully revealed the Vajrayana in Tibet, circa 8th – 9th centuries. He is considered an emanation or Nirmāṇakāya of Shakyamuni Buddha as foretold by the Buddha himself. According to early Tibetan sources including the Testament of Ba, he came to Tibet in the 8th century and designed Samye Monastery, the first Buddhist monastery in Tibet, during the reign of King Trisong Detsen. He, the king, and Khenpo Shantarakshita are also responsible for creating the Tibetan Canon through translating all of the Buddha's teachings and their commentaries into the Tibetan language.

teachers.name: Paramahansa Yogananda
teachers.shortBio: Paramahansa Yogananda was an Indian and American Hindu monk, yogi, and guru who founded the Self-Realization Fellowship (SRF)/Yogoda Satsanga Society of India (YSS), a religious me
biography: Paramahansa Yogananda was an Indian and American Hindu monk, yogi, and guru who founded the Self-Realization Fellowship (SRF)/Yogoda Satsanga Society of India (YSS), a religious meditation and Kriya Yoga organization, to disseminate his teachings. A chief disciple of the yoga guru Swami Sri Yukteswar Giri, he was sent by his lineage to spread yogic teachings to the West. He immigrated to the US at the age of 27, intending to demonstrate a unity between Eastern and Western religions and advocate for a balance between Western material growth and Indian spirituality. His longstanding influence on the American yoga movement, and especially the yoga culture of Los Angeles, led yoga experts to consider him the "Father of Yoga in the West". He lived his final 32 years in the US.

teachers.name: Paul Hedderman
teachers.shortBio: Paul Hedderman is a spiritual teacher known for his direct, no-nonsense approach to non-duality, emphasizing the dissolution of the illusion of a separate self.
biography: Paul Hedderman offers a direct, no-nonsense style of non-dual pointing rooted in his background in recovery and lived inquiry.
His talks and writings emphasize seeing through the illusion of a separate self with humor, clarity, and uncompromising directness.

teachers.name: Pema Chödrön
teachers.shortBio: Pema Chödrön is an American-born Tibetan Buddhist. She is an ordained nun, former acharya of Shambhala Buddhism and disciple of Chögyam Trungpa Rinpoche. Chödrön has written severa
biography: Pema Chödrön is an American-born Tibetan Buddhist. She is an ordained nun, former acharya of Shambhala Buddhism and disciple of Chögyam Trungpa Rinpoche. Chödrön has written several dozen books and audiobooks, and was principal teacher at Gampo Abbey in Nova Scotia. She retired in 2020.

teachers.name: Pema Khandro
teachers.shortBio: Pema Khandro is an American lama, scholar, and lineage holder in the Nyingma and Kagyu ngakpa traditions, specializing in Dzogchen, Chöd, and the history of Tibetan yogis.
biography: Pema Khandro is an authorized lama and lineage holder recognized as a tulku by Gyaldak Rinpoche. She ordained in the Nyingma ngakpa tradition and was authorized to transmit the non-monastic lineage of Buddhist yogis to her students.

She holds a PhD in Buddhist Studies from the University of Virginia, specializing in Dzogchen and women in Buddhism. She founded Ngakpa International, the Buddhist Studies Institute, Dakini Mountain Retreat Center, and the Yogic Medicine Institute.

teachers.name: Pernille & Todd Lent Damore
teachers.shortBio: Pernille and Todd Lent Damore are contemporary spiritual teachers who teach an integrated, experiential path to awakening using the Buddhist framework of the Ten Fetters.
biography: Pernille and Todd Lent Damore offer a structured, experiential approach to awakening grounded in the Buddhist model of the Ten Fetters.
Their teaching integrates inquiry, meditation, and lived practice into a clear map for recognizing and releasing the bonds that sustain the sense of a separate self.

teachers.name: Philip Kapleau
teachers.shortBio: Philip Kapleau was an American Zen Buddhist teacher. He trained in the Harada–Yasutani tradition, which is rooted in Japanese Sōtō and incorporates Rinzai-school koan study. He est
biography: Philip Kapleau was an American Zen Buddhist teacher. He trained in the Harada–Yasutani tradition, which is rooted in Japanese Sōtō and incorporates Rinzai-school koan study. He established Rochester Zen Center, which grew to become one of the most influential Zen communities in the West. His independent lineage includes teachers active in the USA, Canada, Costa Rica, Mexico, Sweden, Finland, Germany, the UK and New Zealand.

bibliography.title: The Zen of living and dying

teachers.name: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin
teachers.shortBio: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin, S.J. was a French Jesuit Catholic priest, scientist, paleontologist, philosopher, mystic, and teacher. Teilhard de Chardin investigated the theory of ev
biography: Pierre Teilhard de Chardin, S.J. was a French Jesuit Catholic priest, scientist, paleontologist, philosopher, mystic, and teacher. Teilhard de Chardin investigated the theory of evolution from a perspective influenced by Henri Bergson and Christian mysticism, writing multiple scientific and religious works on the subject. His mainstream scientific achievements include his paleontological research in China, taking part in the discovery of the significant Peking Man fossils from the Zhoukoudian cave complex near Beijing. His more speculative ideas, sometimes criticized as pseudoscientific, have included a vitalist conception of the Omega Point. Along with Vladimir Vernadsky, he contributed to the development of the concept of the noosphere.

teachers.name: Rabia of Basra
teachers.shortBio: Rābiʼa al-ʼAdawiyya al-Qaysiyya or Rabia Basri was a poet, one of the earliest Sufi mystics and an influential religious figure from Basra. Her full name is said to be Rābiʿa bint
biography: Rābiʼa al-ʼAdawiyya al-Qaysiyya or Rabia Basri was a poet, one of the earliest Sufi mystics and an influential religious figure from Basra. Her full name is said to be Rābiʿa bint Ismāʿīl; her kunya was Umm ʿAmr, her wilāya was al-ʿAtakiyyah, and her nisba was al-ʿAdawiyya or al-Qaysīyya because she was a freed slave of the tribe of Qays ibn ʿAdī. She is regarded as one of the three preeminent Qalandars of the world.

teachers.name: Ram Dass
teachers.shortBio: Ram Dass, also known as Baba Ram Dass, was an American spiritual teacher, guru of modern yoga, psychologist, and writer. His best-selling 1971 book Be Here Now, which has been desc
biography: Ram Dass, also known as Baba Ram Dass, was an American spiritual teacher, guru of modern yoga, psychologist, and writer. His best-selling 1971 book Be Here Now, which has been described by multiple reviewers as "seminal", helped popularize Eastern spirituality and yoga in the West. He authored or co-authored twelve more books on spirituality over the next four decades, including Grist for the Mill (1977), How Can I Help? (1985), and Polishing the Mirror (2013).

teachers.name: Rama Tirtha
teachers.location: presidencies and provinces in British India
teachers.shortBio: Swami Rama Tirtha, also known as Swami Rama, was an Indian teacher of the Hindu philosophy of Vedanta. He was among the first notable teachers of Hinduism to lecture in the United
biography: Swami Rama Tirtha, also known as Swami Rama, was an Indian teacher of the Hindu philosophy of Vedanta. He was among the first notable teachers of Hinduism to lecture in the United States, travelling there in 1902, preceded by Swami Vivekananda in 1893 and followed by Paramahansa Yogananda in 1920. During his American tours Swami Rama Tirtha spoke frequently on the concept of "practical Vedanta" and education of Indian youth. He proposed bringing young Indians to American universities and helped establish scholarships for Indian students.

bibliography.title: Heart of Rama; or, a Collection of the Instructive Teachings of Swami Rama Tirtha

teachers.name: Ramakrishna
teachers.shortBio: Ramakrishna, also called Ramakrishna Paramahamsa, born Ramakrishna Chattopadhyay, was an Indian Hindu mystic. He was a devotee of the goddess Kali, but adhered to various religious
biography: Ramakrishna, also called Ramakrishna Paramahamsa, born Ramakrishna Chattopadhyay, was an Indian Hindu mystic. He was a devotee of the goddess Kali, but adhered to various religious practices from the Hindu traditions of Vaishnavism, Tantric Shaktism, and Advaita Vedanta, as well as Christianity and Sufi Islam. His parable-based teachings advocated the essential unity of religions and proclaimed that world religions are "so many paths to reach one and the same goal". He is regarded by his followers as an avatar.

biography: Dzogchen Ranyak Patrul Rinpoche is a Tibetan lama, teacher, and author in the Nyingma school of Tibetan Buddhism. He is the founder of the Dzogchen Centre Belgium, a branch of the Dzogchen Monastery in Tibet.

biography: Ravi Shankar is an Indian guru and spiritual leader. He is also referred to by the honorifics Sri Sri and Gurudev. From the mid-1970s, he worked as an apprentice under Maharishi Mahesh Yogi, the founder of Transcendental Meditation, before founding the Art of Living Foundation in 1981.

biography: Richard Rohr, is an American Franciscan priest and writer on spirituality based in Albuquerque, New Mexico. He was ordained to the priesthood in the Roman Catholic Church in 1970, founded the New Jerusalem Community in Cincinnati in 1971, and the Center for Action and Contemplation in Albuquerque in 1987. In 2011, PBS called him "one of the most popular spirituality authors and speakers in the world".

biography: Robert Adams was an American Advaita teacher. In later life, Adams held satsang with a small group of devotees in California, USA. He mainly advocated the path of jñāna yoga with an emphasis on the practice of self-enquiry.

teachers.name: Robert Baker Aitken
teachers.shortBio: Robert Baker Dairyu Chotan Aitken Rōshi was a Zen teacher in the Harada-Yasutani lineage. He co-founded the Honolulu Diamond Sangha in 1959 with his wife, Anne Hopkins Aitken. Aitk
biography: Robert Baker Dairyu Chotan Aitken Rōshi was a Zen teacher in the Harada-Yasutani lineage. He co-founded the Honolulu Diamond Sangha in 1959 with his wife, Anne Hopkins Aitken. Aitken received Dharma transmission from Koun Yamada in 1985 but decided to live as a layperson. He was a socialist and anarchist who advocated for social justice for homosexuals, women and Native Hawaiians throughout his life, and was one of the original founders of the Buddhist Peace Fellowship.

teachers.name: Robert Thurman
teachers.shortBio: Robert Alexander Farrar Thurman is an American Buddhist author and academic who has written, edited, and translated several books on Tibetan Buddhism. He was the Je Tsongkhapa Prof
biography: Robert Alexander Farrar Thurman is an American Buddhist author and academic who has written, edited, and translated several books on Tibetan Buddhism. He was the Je Tsongkhapa Professor of Indo-Tibetan Buddhist Studies at Columbia University, before retiring in June 2019. He was the first endowed chair in Buddhist Studies in the West. He also is the co-founder and president of the Tibet House US New York. He translated the Vimalakirti Sutra from the Tibetan Kanjur into English. He is the father of actress Uma Thurman and grandfather of Maya Hawke.

teachers.name: Rolling Thunder
teachers.shortBio: Rolling Thunder is a 1986 run and gun video game developed and published by Namco for arcades. It was released by Atari Games in North America. The player takes control of a secret
biography: Rolling Thunder is a 1986 run and gun video game developed and published by Namco for arcades. It was released by Atari Games in North America. The player takes control of a secret agent who must rescue his female partner from a terrorist organization. Rolling Thunder was a commercial success in arcades, and was released for various home computer platforms in 1987 and the Nintendo Entertainment System in 1989. The original arcade game has been included in various classic game compilations as well. It influenced later arcade action franchises such as Shinobi and Time Crisis, which borrowed mechanics such as taking cover behind crates.

teachers.name: Rumi
teachers.shortBio: Jalāl al-Dīn Muḥammad Rūmī, commonly known as Rumi, was a Sufi mystic, poet, and founder of the Islamic brotherhood known as the Mevlevi Order. His family hailed from Balkh. Rumi i
biography: Jalāl al-Dīn Muḥammad Rūmī, commonly known as Rumi, was a Sufi mystic, poet, and founder of the Islamic brotherhood known as the Mevlevi Order. His family hailed from Balkh. Rumi is an influential figure in Sufism, and his thought and works loom large both in Persian literature and mystic poetry in general. Today, his translated works are enjoyed all over the world.

teachers.name: Ruth Denison
teachers.shortBio: Ruth Denison was the first Buddhist teacher in the United States to lead an all-women's retreat for Buddhist meditation and instruction. Her center, Dhamma Dena Desert Vipassana Ce
biography: Ruth Denison was the first Buddhist teacher in the United States to lead an all-women's retreat for Buddhist meditation and instruction. Her center, Dhamma Dena Desert Vipassana Center is located in the Mojave Desert, in Joshua Tree, California. She was also a teacher at the Insight Meditation Society in Barre, Massachusetts. She sometimes taught at Spirit Rock Meditation Center in Woodacre, California.

teachers.name: S. N. Goenka
teachers.shortBio: Satya Narayana Goenka was an Indian teacher of vipassanā meditation. Born in Burma to an Indian family, he learnt Vipassana from Sayagyi U Ba Khin, retired from business in 1962 du
biography: Satya Narayana Goenka was an Indian teacher of vipassanā meditation. Born in Burma to an Indian family, he learnt Vipassana from Sayagyi U Ba Khin, retired from business in 1962 during business nationalization by military government in Burma, and moved to India in 1969 to start teaching Vipassana meditation. His teachings stay away from rites and rituals and emphasize that Buddha's path to liberation was non-sectarian, universal, and scientific in character, leading to Vipassana meditation appealing to people of all religions as well as no religion, from all parts of the world. He became an influential teacher and played an important role in establishing non-commercial Vipassana meditation centers globally where Vipassana Meditation is taught as a 10 Day residential program with no charges for food, stay as well as for teaching meditation, with centers funded by willful donations from past meditators. He was awarded the Padma Bhushan by the Government of India in 2012, an award given for distinguished service of high order.

bibliography.title: Resumen de las Charlas del Curso de Diez Días

teachers.name: Sadhguru
teachers.shortBio: Jagadish "Jaggi" Vasudev, also known as Sadhguru, is an Indian guru and founder of the Isha Foundation, based in Coimbatore, India. The foundation, established in 1992, operates an
biography: Jagadish "Jaggi" Vasudev, also known as Sadhguru, is an Indian guru and founder of the Isha Foundation, based in Coimbatore, India. The foundation, established in 1992, operates an ashram and yoga centre that carries out educational and spiritual activities. Sadhguru has been teaching yoga since 1982. He is the author of the New York Times bestsellers Inner Engineering: A Yogi's Guide to Joy and Karma: A Yogi's Guide to Crafting Your Destiny, and a frequent speaker at international forums.

teachers.shortBio: Sakyong Jamgon Mipham Rinpoche, Jampal Trinley Dradül, born Ösel Rangdröl Mukpo, is a Tibetan Buddhist master and holder of the Sakyong Lineage of Mukpodong, his family lineage. Th
biography: Sakyong Jamgon Mipham Rinpoche, Jampal Trinley Dradül, born Ösel Rangdröl Mukpo, is a Tibetan Buddhist master and holder of the Sakyong Lineage of Mukpodong, his family lineage. The Sakyong was recognized by Penor Rinpoche in 1995 as the tülku (reincarnation) of Ju Mipham Gyatso, a Rimé teacher of the late 19th century who said he would be reborn only in the legendary Kingdom of Shambhala.

teachers.name: Satchidananda Saraswati
teachers.shortBio: Satchidananda Saraswati, born C. K. Ramaswamy Gounder and known as Swami Satchidananda, was an Indian yoga guru and religious teacher, who gained following in the West. He founded
biography: Satchidananda Saraswati, born C. K. Ramaswamy Gounder and known as Swami Satchidananda, was an Indian yoga guru and religious teacher, who gained following in the West. He founded his own brand of Integral Yoga, and its Yogaville headquarters in Virginia. He was the author of philosophical and spiritual books and had a core of founding disciples who compiled his translations and updated commentaries on traditional handbooks of yoga such as the Yoga Sutras of Patanjali and the Bhagavad Gita for modern readers.

teachers.name: Sayadaw U Pandita
teachers.shortBio: Sayadaw U Paṇḍita was one of the foremost masters of Vipassanā. He trained in the Theravada Buddhist tradition of Myanmar. A successor to the late Mahāsi Sayādaw, he has taught man
biography: Sayadaw U Paṇḍita was one of the foremost masters of Vipassanā. He trained in the Theravada Buddhist tradition of Myanmar. A successor to the late Mahāsi Sayādaw, he has taught many of the Western teachers and students of the Mahāsi style of Vipassanā meditation. He was the abbot of Paṇḍitārāma Meditation Center in Yangon, Myanmar.

biography: Seraphim Rose, also known as Seraphim of Platina, was an American priest and hieromonk of the Russian Orthodox Church Outside Russia who co-founded the Saint Herman of Alaska Monastery in Platina, California. He translated Eastern Orthodox Christian texts and authored several works. His writings have been credited with helping to spread Eastern Orthodox Christianity throughout the West; his popularity equally extended to Russia itself, where his works were secretly reproduced and distributed by samizdat during the Communist era, remaining popular today.

bibliography.title: Orthodoxy and the Religion of the Future

teachers.name: Seung Sahn
teachers.shortBio: Seungsahn Haengwon, born Duk-In Lee, was a Korean Seon master of the Jogye Order and founder of the international Kwan Um School of Zen. He was the seventy-eighth Patriarch in his
biography: Seungsahn Haengwon, born Duk-In Lee, was a Korean Seon master of the Jogye Order and founder of the international Kwan Um School of Zen. He was the seventy-eighth Patriarch in his lineage. As one of the early Korean Zen masters to settle in the United States, he opened many temples and practice groups across the globe. He was known for his charismatic style and direct presentation of Zen, which was well tailored for the Western audience.

bibliography.title: Whole World S a Single Flower

teachers.name: Sharon Salzberg
teachers.shortBio: Sharon Salzberg is an author and teacher of Buddhist meditation practice in the West. In 1974, she co-founded the Insight Meditation Society at Barre, Massachusetts, with Jack Korn
biography: Sharon Salzberg is an author and teacher of Buddhist meditation practice in the West. In 1974, she co-founded the Insight Meditation Society at Barre, Massachusetts, with Jack Kornfield and Joseph Goldstein. Her emphasis is on vipassanā (insight) and mettā (loving-kindness) methods, and she has been leading meditation retreats around the world for several decades.

teachers.name: Shechen Rabjam Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Shechen Rabjam Rinpoche is the seventh Shechen Rabjam and abbot of Shechen Tennyi Dargyeling Monastery, a principal holder of the Nyingma and Dzogchen transmissions of Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche.
biography: Shechen Rabjam Rinpoche was born in 1966 as the grandson and spiritual heir of Dilgo Khyentse Rinpoche. He was recognized as the seventh incarnation of Shechen Rabjam and enthroned at Shechen Monastery in Nepal.

As abbot of Shechen Tennyi Dargyeling, he oversees monastic education, publishing, and humanitarian projects. He teaches the Khyentse and Shechen lineages of Dzogchen, Mahamudra, and Buddhist philosophy worldwide.

teachers.name: Sheng Yen
teachers.shortBio: Sheng Yen, born Zhang Baokang, was a Taiwanese Buddhist monk, religious scholar, and writer. He was one of the mainstream teachers of Chan Buddhism. He was a 57th generational dhar
biography: Sheng Yen, born Zhang Baokang, was a Taiwanese Buddhist monk, religious scholar, and writer. He was one of the mainstream teachers of Chan Buddhism. He was a 57th generational dharma heir of Linji Yixuan in the Linji school and a third-generation dharma heir of Hsu Yun. In the Caodong lineage, Sheng Yen was a 52nd-generation Dharma heir of Dongshan Liangjie (807-869), and a direct Dharma heir of Dongchu (1908–1977).

teachers.name: Shunryu Suzuki
teachers.shortBio: Shunryu Suzuki was a Sōtō Zen monk and teacher who helped popularize Zen Buddhism in the United States, and is renowned for founding the first Zen Buddhist monastery outside Asia.
biography: Shunryu Suzuki was a Sōtō Zen monk and teacher who helped popularize Zen Buddhism in the United States, and is renowned for founding the first Zen Buddhist monastery outside Asia. Suzuki founded San Francisco Zen Center which, along with its affiliate temples, comprises one of the most influential Zen organizations in the United States. A book of his teachings, Zen Mind, Beginner's Mind, is one of the most popular books on Zen and Buddhism in the West.

teachers.name: Simon Mundie
teachers.shortBio: Simon Mundie is a coach, author, and host of the podcast A New Way of Being, who explores non-duality and peak performance through dialogue with thinkers, athletes, and spiritual teachers.
biography: Simon Mundie is a coach, author, and host of the podcast A New Way of Being, bridging non-duality with everyday life and performance.
Through interviews and teaching he explores how recognizing awareness transforms identity, purpose, and the experience of being human.

teachers.name: Sivananda Saraswati
teachers.shortBio: Swami Sivananda Saraswati, also called Swami Sivananda, was a yoga guru, a Hindu spiritual teacher, and a proponent of Vedanta. Sivananda was born in Pattamadai, in the Tirunelveli
biography: Swami Sivananda Saraswati, also called Swami Sivananda, was a yoga guru, a Hindu spiritual teacher, and a proponent of Vedanta. Sivananda was born in Pattamadai, in the Tirunelveli district of modern Tamil Nadu, and was named Kuppuswami. He studied medicine and served in British Malaya as a physician for several years before taking up monasticism.

bibliography.title: Tantra Yoga Nada Yoga Kriya Yoga
Sure Ways for Success in Life and God-Realisation

teachers.name: Sobonfu Somé
teachers.shortBio: Sobonfu Somé was a Burkinabe teacher and writer, specializing in topics of spirituality. She wrote three books: her first, The Spirit of Intimacy, looks at relationships and intima
biography: Sobonfu Somé was a Burkinabe teacher and writer, specializing in topics of spirituality. She wrote three books: her first, The Spirit of Intimacy, looks at relationships and intimacy through the lens of African spirituality and teachings.

bibliography.title: Women's Wisdom from the Heart of Africa
Enseñanzas Africanas Sobre El Amor y La Amistad

teachers.name: Sogyal Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Sogyal Rinpoche was a Tibetan Dzogchen lama. He was recognized as the incarnation of a Tibetan master and visionary saint of the 20th century, Tertön Sogyal Lerab Lingpa. Sogyal Ri
biography: Sogyal Rinpoche was a Tibetan Dzogchen lama. He was recognized as the incarnation of a Tibetan master and visionary saint of the 20th century, Tertön Sogyal Lerab Lingpa. Sogyal Rinpoche was the founder and former spiritual director of Rigpa — an international network of over 100 Buddhist centres and groups in 23 countries around the world — and the author of the best-selling book The Tibetan Book of Living and Dying, which has been printed in 30 languages and 56 countries. Before his retirement, in the wake of abuse allegations in 2017, he had been teaching for 40 years in Europe, America, Asia and Australia.

bibliography.title: Tibetan Book of Living and Dying el Libro Tibetano de la Vida y de la Muerte
Libro Tibetano de la Vida y de la Muerte

teachers.name: Sophrony Sakharov
teachers.shortBio: Sophrony the Athonite, known also as Saint Sophrony, Elder Sophrony or Father Sophrony, was a Russian-born Orthodox Christian archimandrite and one of the most noted ascetic monks
biography: Sophrony the Athonite, known also as Saint Sophrony, Elder Sophrony or Father Sophrony, was a Russian-born Orthodox Christian archimandrite and one of the most noted ascetic monks of the 20th century. He is best known as the disciple and biographer of Silouan the Athonite and compiler of his works in the classic book Saint Silouan the Athonite, and also as the founder of the Patriarchal Stavropegic Monastery of St. John the Baptist in Tolleshunt Knights, Maldon, Essex, England.

bibliography.title: We Shall See Him As He Is

teachers.name: Sri Aurobindo
teachers.shortBio: Sri Aurobindo was an Indian philosopher, yogi and nationalist, who is noted for his participation in the movement for India's independence from British rule, he advocated for compl
biography: Sri Aurobindo was an Indian philosopher, yogi and nationalist, who is noted for his participation in the movement for India's independence from British rule, he advocated for complete autonomy through his writings and political activity. Following his imprisonment and acquittal, he withdrew to Pondicherry to devote himself to spiritual practice, eventually formulating the path of Integral Yoga. His extensive literary output includes the treatises The Life Divine and The Synthesis of Yoga and the epic poem Savitri.

bibliography.title: The hidden forces of life : selections from the works of Sri Aurobindo and The Mother

teachers.name: Sridhar Rana Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Sridhar Rana Rinpoche is a Nepalese Nyingma lama and Dzogchen teacher who studied under Chatral Rinpoche and other masters of the non-monastic yogic tradition.
biography: Sridhar Rana Rinpoche was born in Nepal and trained under Chatral Sangye Dorje Rinpoche and other Nyingma masters, receiving the complete transmissions of sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen.
He teaches the Great Perfection and supports Dharma activities in Nepal and internationally, emphasizing direct practice and retreat.

teachers.name: Stephen Batchelor
teachers.shortBio: Stephen Batchelor is a Scottish Buddhist author and teacher, known for his writings on Buddhist subjects and his leadership of meditation retreats worldwide. He is a noted proponen
biography: Stephen Batchelor is a Scottish Buddhist author and teacher, known for his writings on Buddhist subjects and his leadership of meditation retreats worldwide. He is a noted proponent of agnostic or secular Buddhism.

teachers.name: Sun Bear
teachers.shortBio: The sun bear is a bear species in the family Ursidae found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is the only species in the genus Helarctos and the smallest bear species, s
biography: The sun bear is a bear species in the family Ursidae found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is the only species in the genus Helarctos and the smallest bear species, standing nearly 70 cm (28 in) at the shoulder and weighing 25–65 kg (55–143 lb). It is stockily built, with large paws, strongly curved claws, small, rounded ears and a short snout. The fur is generally short and jet black, but can vary from grey to red. The sun bear gets its name from its characteristic orange to cream-coloured chest patch.

teachers.name: Sunny Sharma
teachers.shortBio: Sunny Sharma is a spiritual teacher who explores non-duality and self-realization, guiding individuals toward direct recognition of their true nature beyond the mind and ego.
biography: Sunny Sharma explores non-duality and self-realization through teaching and dialogue that points beyond mind and ego.
The work invites a direct recognition of true nature already present prior to conceptual understanding.

bibliography.title: Advances in Growth Regulation of Fruit Crops

teachers.name: Suzanne Chang
teachers.shortBio: Suzanne Chang is a non-duality teacher who gently points to the illusion of the separate self, inviting a clear and simple recognition of the natural freedom and wholeness of life as it is.
biography: Suzanne Chang offers gentle, direct pointing to the illusion of the separate self and the natural wholeness of life as it is.
Her style emphasizes simplicity and clarity, inviting recognition of freedom without effort or spiritual striving.

teachers.name: Swami Prabhupada
teachers.shortBio: Abhay Charanaravinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada was an Indian spiritual, philosophical, and religious teacher who spread the Hare Krishna mantra and the teachings of "Krishna c
biography: Abhay Charanaravinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada was an Indian spiritual, philosophical, and religious teacher who spread the Hare Krishna mantra and the teachings of "Krishna consciousness" to the world. Born as Abhay Charan De and later legally named Abhay Charanaravinda Bhaktivedanta Swami, he is often referred to as "Bhaktivedanta Swami", "Srila Prabhupada", or simply "Prabhupada".

bibliography.title: Bhagvad Gita As It Is English New Edition

teachers.name: Swami Rama
teachers.shortBio: Swami Rama was an Indian yoga guru. He moved to the US in 1969, initially teaching yoga at the YMCA, and founding the Himalayan Institute of Yoga Science and Philosophy in Illinois
biography: Swami Rama was an Indian yoga guru. He moved to the US in 1969, initially teaching yoga at the YMCA, and founding the Himalayan Institute of Yoga Science and Philosophy in Illinois in 1971; its headquarters moved to its current location in Honesdale, Pennsylvania in 1977. He became famous for his ability to control his body in yoga nidra, writing many books including the autobiographical Living with Himalayan Masters. From the 1970s onwards, there were persistent allegations of sexual abuse of his followers; in 1997 a woman won a lawsuit against him for multiple sexual assaults.

bibliography.title: Heart of Rama; or, a Collection of the Instructive Teachings of Swami Rama Tirtha
Perennial psychology of the Bhagavad Gita

teachers.name: Swami Sarvapriyananda
teachers.shortBio: Swami Sarvapriyananda is a Hindu monk (sannyasi) belonging to the Ramakrishna Order. He is the current resident Swami and Minister in Charge, of the Vedanta Society of New York, a
biography: Swami Sarvapriyananda is a Hindu monk (sannyasi) belonging to the Ramakrishna Order. He is the current resident Swami and Minister in Charge, of the Vedanta Society of New York, a position he has been serving since January 2017.

teachers.name: Swami Vivekananda
teachers.location: Swami Vivekananda's Ancestral House & Cultural Centre
teachers.shortBio: Swami Vivekananda, born Narendranath Datta, was an Indian Hindu monk, philosopher, author, religious teacher, and the chief disciple of the Indian mystic Ramakrishna. Vivekananda w
biography: Swami Vivekananda, born Narendranath Datta, was an Indian Hindu monk, philosopher, author, religious teacher, and the chief disciple of the Indian mystic Ramakrishna. Vivekananda was a major figure in the introduction of Vedanta and Yoga to the Western world, and is credited with raising interfaith awareness and elevating Hinduism to the status of a major world religion.

teachers.name: Sylvia Boorstein
teachers.shortBio: Sylvia Boorstein is an American author, psychotherapist, and Buddhist teacher.
biography: Sylvia Boorstein is an American author, psychotherapist, and Buddhist teacher.

teachers.name: Symeon the New Theologian
teachers.shortBio: Saint Symeon the New Theologian was an Eastern Orthodox monk and poet who was one of the three saints canonized by the Eastern Orthodox Church and given the title of "Theologian".
biography: Saint Symeon the New Theologian was an Eastern Orthodox monk and poet who was one of the three saints canonized by the Eastern Orthodox Church and given the title of "Theologian". "Theologian" was not applied to Symeon in the modern academic sense of theological study; the title was intended only to recognise someone who spoke from personal experience of the vision of God. One of his principal teachings was that humans could and should experience theoria.

teachers.name: Taizan Maezumi
teachers.shortBio: Hakuyū Taizan Maezumi was a Japanese Sōtō Zen Buddhist priest who substantially contributed to development of Zen in the United States.
biography: Hakuyū Taizan Maezumi was a Japanese Sōtō Zen Buddhist priest who substantially contributed to development of Zen in the United States.

bibliography.title: El valor de la vida/ Appreciate Your Life (El Viaje Interior/ the Interior Voyage)

teachers.name: Tara Brach
teachers.shortBio: Tara Brach is an American psychologist, author, and proponent of Buddhist meditation. She is a senior teacher and founder of the Insight Meditation Community of Washington, D.C. (I
biography: Tara Brach is an American psychologist, author, and proponent of Buddhist meditation. She is a senior teacher and founder of the Insight Meditation Community of Washington, D.C. (IMCW). Brach also teaches about Buddhist meditation at centers for meditation and yoga in the United States and Europe, including Spirit Rock Meditation Center in Woodacre, California; the Kripalu Center; and the Omega Institute for Holistic Studies.

bibliography.title: Buda'nin Kalbi; Korkuyu ve Utanci Iyilestiren Sefkati Uyandirmak

teachers.name: Tenzin Gyatso
teachers.shortBio: The 14th Dalai Lama is the incumbent Dalai Lama, the highest spiritual leader and head of Tibetan Buddhism. He served as the resident spiritual and temporal leader of Tibet before
biography: The 14th Dalai Lama is the incumbent Dalai Lama, the highest spiritual leader and head of Tibetan Buddhism. He served as the resident spiritual and temporal leader of Tibet before 1959, and subsequently led the Tibetan government in exile represented by the Central Tibetan Administration in Dharamshala, India.
