teachers.name: Tenzin Palmo
teachers.shortBio: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo is a bhikṣuṇī in the Drukpa Kagyu lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. She is an author, teacher and founder of Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery in Hima
biography: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo is a bhikṣuṇī in the Drukpa Kagyu lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. She is an author, teacher and founder of Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery in Himachal Pradesh, India. She is best known for being one of the few Western yoginis trained in the East, having spent twelve years living in a remote cave in the Himalayas, three of those years in strict meditation retreat.

teachers.name: Tenzin Wangyal Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tenzin Wangyal Rinpoche is a teacher (lama) of the Bon Tibetan religious tradition. He is founder and director of the Ligmincha Institute and several centers named Chamma Ling, org
biography: Tenzin Wangyal Rinpoche is a teacher (lama) of the Bon Tibetan religious tradition. He is founder and director of the Ligmincha Institute and several centers named Chamma Ling, organizations dedicated to the study and practice of the teachings of the Bon tradition.

bibliography.title: Tibetan Yogas of Dream and Sleep

teachers.name: Teresa of Ávila
teachers.shortBio: Teresa of Ávila, religious name Teresa of Jesus, was a Carmelite nun, prominent Spanish mystic and spiritual reformer.
biography: Teresa of Ávila, religious name Teresa of Jesus, was a Carmelite nun, prominent Spanish mystic and spiritual reformer.

bibliography.title: The collected works of St. Teresa of Avila

teachers.name: Thanissaro Bhikkhu
teachers.shortBio: Ṭhānissaro Bhikkhu is an American Buddhist monk and author. Belonging to the Thai Forest Tradition, he studied for ten years under the forest master Ajahn Fuang Jotiko. Since 1993,
biography: Ṭhānissaro Bhikkhu is an American Buddhist monk and author. Belonging to the Thai Forest Tradition, he studied for ten years under the forest master Ajahn Fuang Jotiko. Since 1993, he has served as abbot of the Metta Forest Monastery in San Diego County, California—the first monastery in the Thai Forest Tradition in the U.S.—which he cofounded with Ajahn Suwat Suvaco.

teachers.name: Thérèse of Lisieux
teachers.shortBio: Thérèse of Lisieux, religious name Therese of the Child Jesus and the Holy Face, was a French Discalced Carmelite who is widely venerated in modern times. She is popularly known in
biography: Thérèse of Lisieux, religious name Therese of the Child Jesus and the Holy Face, was a French Discalced Carmelite who is widely venerated in modern times. She is popularly known in English as the "Little Flower of Jesus", or simply the "Little Flower", and in French as la petite Thérèse.

teachers.name: Thich Nhat Hanh
teachers.shortBio: Thích Nhất Hạnh was a Vietnamese Thiền Buddhist monk, peace activist, prolific author, poet, and teacher, who founded the Plum Village Tradition, historically recognized as the mai
biography: Thích Nhất Hạnh was a Vietnamese Thiền Buddhist monk, peace activist, prolific author, poet, and teacher, who founded the Plum Village Tradition, historically recognized as the main inspiration for engaged Buddhism. Known as the "father of mindfulness", Nhất Hạnh was a major influence on Western practices of Buddhism.

bibliography.title: The heart of the Buddha's teaching

teachers.name: Thomas à Kempis
teachers.shortBio: Thomas à Kempis was a German-Dutch Catholic canon regular of the Augustinians and the author of The Imitation of Christ, one of the best known Christian devotional books. His name
biography: Thomas à Kempis was a German-Dutch Catholic canon regular of the Augustinians and the author of The Imitation of Christ, one of the best known Christian devotional books. His name means "Thomas of Kempen", Kempen, Germany, being his home town.

bibliography.title: The Imitation of Christ by Thomas A. Kempis (A New Reading of the 1441 Latin Autograph Manuscript)
The eucharistic jewels of à Kempis

biography: Thomas Banyacya, Sr. was a Hopi Native American traditionalist leader.

teachers.shortBio: Thomas Keating, O.C.S.O. was an American Trappist priest known as one of the principal developers of centering prayer, a contemplative method that emerged from St. Joseph's Abbey i
biography: Thomas Keating, O.C.S.O. was an American Trappist priest known as one of the principal developers of centering prayer, a contemplative method that emerged from St. Joseph's Abbey in Spencer, Massachusetts.

bibliography.title: Fruits and gifts of the Spirit

teachers.name: Thomas Merton
teachers.shortBio: Thomas Merton, religious name M. Louis, was an American Trappist monk, theologian, mystic, poet, and social activist. He was a professed member of the Abbey of Our Lady of Gethsema
biography: Thomas Merton, religious name M. Louis, was an American Trappist monk, theologian, mystic, poet, and social activist. He was a professed member of the Abbey of Our Lady of Gethsemani, near Bardstown, Kentucky, living there from 1941 to his death.

teachers.name: Thrangu Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Thrangu Rinpoche was a tulku in the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism, the ninth reincarnation in his particular line. His full name and title was the Very Venerable Ninth Khenchen
biography: Thrangu Rinpoche was a tulku in the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism, the ninth reincarnation in his particular line. His full name and title was the Very Venerable Ninth Khenchen Thrangu Tulku, Karma Lodrö Lungrik Maway Senge. The academic title Khenchen denotes great scholarly accomplishment, and the term Rinpoche is a Tibetan devotional title which may be accorded to respected teachers and exemplars.

An Ocean of the Ultimate Meaning

teachers.name: Thubten Chodron
teachers.shortBio: Thubten Chodron, born Cheryl Greene, is an American Tibetan Buddhist nun, author, teacher, and the founder and abbess of Sravasti Abbey, the only Tibetan Buddhist training monaster
biography: Thubten Chodron, born Cheryl Greene, is an American Tibetan Buddhist nun, author, teacher, and the founder and abbess of Sravasti Abbey, the only Tibetan Buddhist training monastery for Western nuns and monks in the United States. Chodron is a central figure in the reinstatement of the Bhikshuni ordination of women. She is a student of the 14th Dalai Lama, Tsenzhab Serkong Rinpoche, Lama Thubten Yeshe, Thubten Zopa Rinpoche, and other Tibetan masters. She has published many books on Buddhist philosophy and meditation, and is co-authoring with the Dalai Lama a multi-volume series of teachings on the Buddhist path, The Library of Wisdom and Compassion.

teachers.name: Thubten Yeshe
teachers.shortBio: Thubten Yeshe (1935–1984) was a Tibetan lama who, while exiled in Nepal, co-founded Kopan Monastery (1969) and the Foundation for the Preservation of the Mahayana Tradition (1975).
biography: Thubten Yeshe (1935–1984) was a Tibetan lama who, while exiled in Nepal, co-founded Kopan Monastery (1969) and the Foundation for the Preservation of the Mahayana Tradition (1975). He followed the Gelug tradition, and was considered unconventional in his teaching style.

bibliography.title: The peaceful stillness of the silent mind

teachers.name: Toni Packer
teachers.shortBio: Toni Packer was a teacher of “meditative inquiry”, and the founder of Springwater Center. Packer was a former student in the Sanbo Kyodan lineage of Zen Buddhism, and was previousl
biography: Toni Packer was a teacher of “meditative inquiry”, and the founder of Springwater Center. Packer was a former student in the Sanbo Kyodan lineage of Zen Buddhism, and was previously in line to be the successor of Phillip Kapleau at the Rochester Zen Center.

teachers.name: Tony Parsons
teachers.shortBio: Tony Parsons is a contemporary non-duality teacher, known for his uncompromising message that liberation is the simple, ever-present reality beyond the illusion of a separate self.
biography: Tony Parsons is a contemporary non-duality teacher whose uncompromising message points to liberation as the simple, ever-present reality beyond the illusion of a separate self.
His gatherings emphasize that nothing needs to change for wholeness to be recognized, and that the sense of being a seeker is itself part of the illusion.

teachers.name: Traktung Yeshe Dorje
teachers.shortBio: Traktung Khepa Yeshe Dorje is a Western tertön and Dzogchen teacher in the Nyingma tradition, holder of the Ati Zabdön cycle of terma revelations.
biography: Traktung Khepa Yeshe Dorje was recognized within the Nyingma tradition as a tertön and lineage holder. He received and practiced the complete Dzogchen transmissions from his root teachers.
He teaches the Ati Zabdön treasure cycle and Dzogchen pith instructions through Vajrayana Foundation and affiliated communities, emphasizing direct introduction to the nature of mind.

bibliography.title: Eye to form is only love

teachers.name: Tsoknyi Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tsoknyi Rinpoche or Ngawang Tsoknyi Gyatso is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher and author, and the founder of the Pundarika Foundation. He is the third Tsoknyi Rinpoche, having been reco
biography: Tsoknyi Rinpoche or Ngawang Tsoknyi Gyatso is a Tibetan Buddhist teacher and author, and the founder of the Pundarika Foundation. He is the third Tsoknyi Rinpoche, having been recognized by the 16th Karmapa as the reincarnation of Drubwang Tsoknyi Rinpoche. He is a tulku of the Drukpa Kagyü and Nyingma traditions and the holder of the Ratna Lingpa and Tsoknyi lineages., and a son of great master Tulku Urgyen Rinpoche.

teachers.name: Tsongkhapa
teachers.shortBio: Tsongkhapa was an influential Tibetan Buddhist monk, philosopher and tantric yogi, whose activities led to the formation of the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism.
biography: Tsongkhapa was an influential Tibetan Buddhist monk, philosopher and tantric yogi, whose activities led to the formation of the Gelug school of Tibetan Buddhism.

bibliography.title: The Great Treatise on the Stages of the Path to Enlightenment, Volume Two
The Great Treatise on the Stages of the Path to Enlightenment, Volume Three

teachers.name: Tsultrim Allione
teachers.shortBio: Lama Tsultrim Allione is an American author and teacher who has studied and practiced in Tibetan Buddhism's Karma Kagyu lineage as well as in the Nyingma school of Buddhism. She ha
biography: Lama Tsultrim Allione is an American author and teacher who has studied and practiced in Tibetan Buddhism's Karma Kagyu lineage as well as in the Nyingma school of Buddhism. She has been recognised by two different Tibetan Buddhist lamas as an emanation of Machig Labdron, the 11th/12th century CE female founder of several chöd lineages.

bibliography.title: The Mandala of the Enlightened Feminine

teachers.name: Tulku Dakpa Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tulku Dakpa Rinpoche སྤྲུལ་སྐུ་གྲགས་པ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ is a lama in the Nyingma School of Tibetan Buddhism,. He was recognized by His Holiness Mindrolling Trichen Rinpoche as a reincarnat
biography: Tulku Dakpa Rinpoche སྤྲུལ་སྐུ་གྲགས་པ་རིན་པོ་ཆེ is a lama in the Nyingma School of Tibetan Buddhism,. He was recognized by His Holiness Mindrolling Trichen Rinpoche as a reincarnation of Drupwang Rogza Sonam Palge, a hidden yogi of eastern Tibet. He has graduated from the Mindrolling Monastery’s University of Tibetan Buddhism as a certified lineage holder of both sutra and tantra.

bibliography.title: Self-Arisen Vidya Tantra  and the Self-Liberated Vidya Tantra

teachers.name: Tulku Sang-ngag
teachers.shortBio: Tulku Sang-ngag Rinpoche is a Nyingma tertön and founder of Ewam centers, holder of the Namchö cycle and teacher of Dzogchen in the United States and Asia.
biography: Tulku Sang-ngag Rinpoche was recognized as the incarnation of the Gochen Tulku and trained in the Nyingma tradition in Tibet and India. He is a tertön and lineage holder of the Namchö (Space Treasure) cycle revealed by Terton Mingyur Dorje.

He founded Ewam Buddhist Institute in Montana and Ewam centers internationally, teaching Dzogchen, ngöndro, and the Namchö treasure revelations. He is also the visionary behind the Garden of One Thousand Buddhas in Montana.

teachers.name: Tulku Thadral
teachers.shortBio: Tulku Thadral Rinpoche is a Nyingma lama and Dzogchen teacher trained at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche.
biography: Tulku Thadral Rinpoche was recognized as a tulku and completed advanced training at Namdroling Monastery under Penor Rinpoche, receiving the full Nyingma transmissions of sutra, tantra, and Dzogchen.
He teaches ngöndro, sadhana practice, and Dzogchen pith instructions at Palyul and affiliated centers in Asia and the West.

teachers.name: Tulku Thondup Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tulku Thondup Rinpoche is a Nyingma master, scholar, and author who teaches Dzogchen and healing practices through the Living Buddha Dharma Foundation.
biography: Tulku Thondup Rinpoche was born in Golok, Tibet, and recognized as the reincarnation of Khenpo Sherab Gyaltsen. He trained at Dodrupchen Monastery and other Nyingma centers before coming to the West.

He is the author of Masters of Meditation and Miracles, The Healing Power of Mind, and many other books. He teaches Dzogchen, loving-kindness, and healing meditations through the Living Buddha Dharma Foundation at Harvard and worldwide.

teachers.name: Tulku Yeshe Gyatso Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Tulku Yeshe Gyatso Rinpoche is a Nyingma tulku and Dzogchen teacher in the eastern Tibetan tradition.
biography: Tulku Yeshe Gyatso Rinpoche was recognized as a tulku in the Nyingma tradition and trained in sutra, tantra, and the Great Perfection under senior lineage masters.
He teaches Dzogchen and supports monastic communities, transmitting the pith instructions of trekchö and tögal to qualified disciples.

teachers.name: U. G. Krishnamurti
teachers.shortBio: Uppaluri Gopala Krishnamurti was an Indian "anti-guru" who questioned the search for enlightenment. Having pursued a religious path in his youth and eventually rejecting it, U.G. c
biography: Uppaluri Gopala Krishnamurti was an Indian "anti-guru" who questioned the search for enlightenment. Having pursued a religious path in his youth and eventually rejecting it, U.G. clarified that he had experienced a devastating biological transformation on his 49th birthday, an event he referred to as "the calamity". He emphasized that this transformation back to "the natural state" is a rare, acausal, biological occurrence with no religious context. Because of this, he discouraged people from pursuing the "natural state" as a spiritual goal.

teachers.name: Vilayat Inayat Khan
teachers.shortBio: Vilayat Inayat Khan was a teacher of meditation and of the traditions of the East Indian Chishti Sufi Order of Sufism. His teaching derived from the tradition of his father, Inayat
biography: Vilayat Inayat Khan was a teacher of meditation and of the traditions of the East Indian Chishti Sufi Order of Sufism. His teaching derived from the tradition of his father, Inayat Khan, founder of The Sufi Order in the West, in a form tailored to the needs of Western seekers. One of his sisters was Noor Inayat Khan GC MBE. He taught in the tradition of Universal Sufism. His parents met at the New York City ashram of American yogi, Pierre Bernard, half-brother of his mother Pirani Ameena Begum.

teachers.name: Walpola Rahula
teachers.shortBio: Walpola Rahula Thera was a Sri Lankan Buddhist monk, scholar and writer. In 1964, he became the Professor of History and Religions at Northwestern University, thus becoming the fir
biography: Walpola Rahula Thera was a Sri Lankan Buddhist monk, scholar and writer. In 1964, he became the Professor of History and Religions at Northwestern University, thus becoming the first bhikkhu to hold a professorial chair in the Western world. He also once held the position of Vice-Chancellor at the then Vidyodaya University. He has written extensively about Buddhism in English, French and Sinhala. He wrote the book What the Buddha Taught about Theravada Buddhism.

biography: Terence James Stannus Gray was a theatre producer who created the Cambridge Festival Theatre as an experimental theatre in Cambridge. He produced over 100 plays there between 1926 and 1933.
Later in life, under the pen name Wei Wu Wei, he published several books on Taoist philosophy.

teachers.name: Yogi Prabodha Jnana
teachers.shortBio: Yogi Prabodha Jnana is a practitioner and teacher in the Dzogchen tradition, guiding students in meditation and the yogic path of the Great Perfection.
biography: Yogi Prabodha Jnana trained in the Dzogchen tradition under senior lineage masters, completing extensive retreat and practice in the pith instructions of trekchö and tögal.
He teaches meditation and Dzogchen to students in India, emphasizing the direct realization of rigpa and integration of awareness in daily life.

teachers.name: Yogini Abhaya Devi
teachers.shortBio: Yogini Abhaya Devi is a practitioner and teacher in the Dzogchen tradition, offering guidance on meditation and the yogini path of the Great Perfection.
biography: Yogini Abhaya Devi trained in the Dzogchen and Vajrayana traditions, receiving transmissions and pith instructions from senior lineage holders and completing extensive retreat practice.
She teaches meditation and the nature of mind to students, emphasizing the feminine principle in Dzogchen and the integration of wisdom and compassion.

teachers.name: Yongey Mingyur Rinpoche
teachers.shortBio: Yongey Mingyur Rinpoche is a Tibetan Nepali teacher and master of the Karma Kagyu and Nyingma lineages of Tibetan Buddhism. He has written five books, including the 2007 bestseller
biography: Yongey Mingyur Rinpoche is a Tibetan Nepali teacher and master of the Karma Kagyu and Nyingma lineages of Tibetan Buddhism. He has written five books, including the 2007 bestseller The Joy of Living, and oversees the Tergar Meditation Community, an international network of Buddhist meditation centers and programs.

biography: Yunus Emre, also known as Derviş Yûnus, was a Turkish folk poet and Sufi who greatly influenced Turkish culture. The UNESCO General Conference unanimously passed a resolution declaring 1991, the 750th anniversary of the poet's birth, International Yunus Emre Year.

biography: Zia Inayat Khan is a scholar and teacher of Sufism in the lineage of his grandfather, Hazrat Inayat Khan. He is president of the Inayatiyya (formerly Inayati Order) and founder of Suluk Academy, a school of contemplative study with branches in the United States and Europe.
